;;;1433   
;;;1434           if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )

000008  6830              LDR      r0,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000a  4284              CMP      r4,r0
00000c  d102              BNE      |L1.20|
;;;1435           {
;;;1436               /* The task calling this function is querying its own state. */
;;;1437               eReturn = eRunning;
00000e  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L1.16|
;;;1438           }
;;;1439           else
;;;1440           {
;;;1441               taskENTER_CRITICAL();
;;;1442               {
;;;1443                   pxStateList = listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
;;;1444                   pxDelayedList = pxDelayedTaskList;
;;;1445                   pxOverflowedDelayedList = pxOverflowDelayedTaskList;
;;;1446               }
;;;1447               taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
;;;1448   
;;;1449               if( ( pxStateList == pxDelayedList ) || ( pxStateList == pxOverflowedDelayedList ) )
;;;1450               {
;;;1451                   /* The task being queried is referenced from one of the Blocked
;;;1452                    * lists. */
;;;1453                   eReturn = eBlocked;
;;;1454               }
;;;1455   
;;;1456               #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;1457                   else if( pxStateList == &xSuspendedTaskList )
;;;1458                   {
;;;1459                       /* The task being queried is referenced from the suspended
;;;1460                        * list.  Is it genuinely suspended or is it blocked
;;;1461                        * indefinitely? */
;;;1462                       if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == NULL )
;;;1463                       {
;;;1464                           #if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 )
;;;1465                               {
;;;1466                                   BaseType_t x;
;;;1467   
;;;1468                                   /* The task does not appear on the event list item of
;;;1469                                    * and of the RTOS objects, but could still be in the
;;;1470                                    * blocked state if it is waiting on its notification
;;;1471                                    * rather than waiting on an object.  If not, is
;;;1472                                    * suspended. */
;;;1473                                   eReturn = eSuspended;
;;;1474   
;;;1475                                   for( x = 0; x < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES; x++ )
;;;1476                                   {
;;;1477                                       if( pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ x ] == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
;;;1478                                       {
;;;1479                                           eReturn = eBlocked;
;;;1480                                           break;
;;;1481                                       }
;;;1482                                   }
;;;1483                               }
;;;1484                           #else /* if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 ) */
;;;1485                               {
;;;1486                                   eReturn = eSuspended;
;;;1487                               }
;;;1488                           #endif /* if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 ) */
;;;1489                       }
;;;1490                       else
;;;1491                       {
;;;1492                           eReturn = eBlocked;
;;;1493                       }
;;;1494                   }
;;;1495               #endif /* if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 ) */
;;;1496   
;;;1497               #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskDelete == 1 )
;;;1498                   else if( ( pxStateList == &xTasksWaitingTermination ) || ( pxStateList == NULL ) )
;;;1499                   {
;;;1500                       /* The task being queried is referenced from the deleted
;;;1501                        * tasks list, or it is not referenced from any lists at
;;;1502                        * all. */
;;;1503                       eReturn = eDeleted;
;;;1504                   }
;;;1505               #endif
;;;1506   
;;;1507               else /*lint !e525 Negative indentation is intended to make use of pre-processor clearer. */
;;;1508               {
;;;1509                   /* If the task is not in any other state, it must be in the
;;;1510                    * Ready (including pending ready) state. */
;;;1511                   eReturn = eReady;
;;;1512               }
;;;1513           }
;;;1514   
;;;1515           return eReturn;
;;;1516       } /*lint !e818 xTask cannot be a pointer to const because it is a typedef. */

                  |L1.20|

00001a  6c37              LDR      r7,[r6,#0x40]         ;1444  ; pxDelayedTaskList
00001c  6c76              LDR      r6,[r6,#0x44]         ;1445  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList

000024  d001              BEQ      |L1.42|

00002c  e7f0              B        |L1.16|
                  |L1.46|
00002e  480e              LDR      r0,|L1.104|
000030  4285              CMP      r5,r0                 ;1457
000032  d10d              BNE      |L1.80|

000036  2800              CMP      r0,#0                 ;1462

;;;5310   {

;;;5314       #if ( INCLUDE_xTaskAbortDelay == 1 )
;;;5315           {
;;;5316               /* About to enter a delayed list, so ensure the ucDelayAborted flag is
;;;5317                * reset to pdFALSE so it can be detected as having been set to pdTRUE
;;;5318                * when the task leaves the Blocked state. */
;;;5319               pxCurrentTCB->ucDelayAborted = pdFALSE;
;;;5320           }
;;;5321       #endif
;;;5322   
;;;5323       /* Remove the task from the ready list before adding it to the blocked list

000010  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove

;;;5326       {
;;;5327           /* The current task must be in a ready list, so there is no need to
;;;5328            * check, and the port reset macro can be called directly. */
;;;5329           portRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority, uxTopReadyPriority ); /*lint !e931 pxCurrentTCB cannot change as it is the calling task.  pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority and uxTopReadyPriority cannot change as called with scheduler suspended or in a critical section. */

;;;5330       }
;;;5331       else
;;;5332       {
;;;5333           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;5334       }
;;;5335   
;;;5336       #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;5337           {
;;;5338               if( ( xTicksToWait == portMAX_DELAY ) && ( xCanBlockIndefinitely != pdFALSE ) )

000028  d107              BNE      |L3.58|
00002a  b137              CBZ      r7,|L3.58|
;;;5339               {
;;;5340                   /* Add the task to the suspended task list instead of a delayed task
;;;5341                    * list to ensure it is not woken by a timing event.  It will block
;;;5342                    * indefinitely. */
;;;5343                   vListInsertEnd( &xSuspendedTaskList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xStateListItem ) );

000036  f7ffbffe          B.W      vListInsertEnd
                  |L3.58|
;;;5344               }
;;;5345               else
;;;5346               {
;;;5347                   /* Calculate the time at which the task should be woken if the event
;;;5348                    * does not occur.  This may overflow but this doesn't matter, the
;;;5349                    * kernel will manage it correctly. */
;;;5350                   xTimeToWake = xConstTickCount + xTicksToWait;
;;;5351   
;;;5352                   /* The list item will be inserted in wake time order. */
;;;5353                   listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xStateListItem ), xTimeToWake );

00003c  442c              ADD      r4,r4,r5              ;5350
;;;5354   
;;;5355                   if( xTimeToWake < xConstTickCount )
00003e  42ac              CMP      r4,r5
000040  6044              STR      r4,[r0,#4]
;;;5356                   {
;;;5357                       /* Wake time has overflowed.  Place this item in the overflow
;;;5358                        * list. */
;;;5359                       vListInsert( pxOverflowDelayedTaskList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xStateListItem ) );
;;;5360                   }
;;;5361                   else
;;;5362                   {
;;;5363                       /* The wake time has not overflowed, so the current block list
;;;5364                        * is used. */
;;;5365                       vListInsert( pxDelayedTaskList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xStateListItem ) );

000044  d205              BCS      |L3.82|
000046  6c70              LDR      r0,[r6,#0x44]         ;5359  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
000048  e8bd41f0          POP      {r4-r8,lr}            ;5359
00004c  1d09              ADDS     r1,r1,#4              ;5359
00004e  f7ffbffe          B.W      vListInsert
                  |L3.82|
000052  6c30              LDR      r0,[r6,#0x40]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000054  1d09              ADDS     r1,r1,#4
000056  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsert
;;;5366   
;;;5367                       /* If the task entering the blocked state was placed at the
;;;5368                        * head of the list of blocked tasks then xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;5369                        * needs to be updated too. */
;;;5370                       if( xTimeToWake < xNextTaskUnblockTime )
00005a  6ab0              LDR      r0,[r6,#0x28]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
00005c  4284              CMP      r4,r0
00005e  d200              BCS      |L3.98|
;;;5371                       {
;;;5372                           xNextTaskUnblockTime = xTimeToWake;

;;;5373                       }
;;;5374                       else
;;;5375                       {
;;;5376                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;5377                       }
;;;5378                   }
;;;5379               }
;;;5380           }
;;;5381       #else /* INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend */
;;;5382           {
;;;5383               /* Calculate the time at which the task should be woken if the event
;;;5384                * does not occur.  This may overflow but this doesn't matter, the kernel
;;;5385                * will manage it correctly. */
;;;5386               xTimeToWake = xConstTickCount + xTicksToWait;
;;;5387   
;;;5388               /* The list item will be inserted in wake time order. */
;;;5389               listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xStateListItem ), xTimeToWake );
;;;5390   
;;;5391               if( xTimeToWake < xConstTickCount )
;;;5392               {
;;;5393                   /* Wake time has overflowed.  Place this item in the overflow list. */
;;;5394                   vListInsert( pxOverflowDelayedTaskList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xStateListItem ) );
;;;5395               }
;;;5396               else
;;;5397               {
;;;5398                   /* The wake time has not overflowed, so the current block list is used. */
;;;5399                   vListInsert( pxDelayedTaskList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xStateListItem ) );
;;;5400   
;;;5401                   /* If the task entering the blocked state was placed at the head of the
;;;5402                    * list of blocked tasks then xNextTaskUnblockTime needs to be updated
;;;5403                    * too. */
;;;5404                   if( xTimeToWake < xNextTaskUnblockTime )
;;;5405                   {
;;;5406                       xNextTaskUnblockTime = xTimeToWake;
;;;5407                   }
;;;5408                   else
;;;5409                   {
;;;5410                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;5411                   }
;;;5412               }
;;;5413   
;;;5414               /* Avoid compiler warning when INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend is not 1. */
;;;5415               ( void ) xCanBlockIndefinitely;

;;;5417       #endif /* INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend */

;;;5419

                  prvAddNewTaskToReadyList PROC

;;;1113   static void prvAddNewTaskToReadyList( TCB_t * pxNewTCB )

;;;1115       /* Ensure interrupts don't access the task lists while the lists are being
;;;1116        * updated. */
;;;1117       taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000006  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1118       {
;;;1119           uxCurrentNumberOfTasks++;

000016  b110              CBZ      r0,|L4.30|
;;;1122           {
;;;1123               /* There are no other tasks, or all the other tasks are in
;;;1124                * the suspended state - make this the current task. */
;;;1125               pxCurrentTCB = pxNewTCB;
;;;1126   
;;;1127               if( uxCurrentNumberOfTasks == ( UBaseType_t ) 1 )
;;;1128               {
;;;1129                   /* This is the first task to be created so do the preliminary
;;;1130                    * initialisation required.  We will not recover if this call
;;;1131                    * fails, but we will report the failure. */
;;;1132                   prvInitialiseTaskLists();
;;;1133               }
;;;1134               else
;;;1135               {
;;;1136                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1137               }
;;;1138           }
;;;1139           else
;;;1140           {
;;;1141               /* If the scheduler is not already running, make this task the
;;;1142                * current task if it is the highest priority task to be created
;;;1143                * so far. */

;;;1152                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1153                   }
;;;1154               }
;;;1155               else
;;;1156               {
;;;1157                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1158               }
;;;1159           }
;;;1160   
;;;1161           uxTaskNumber++;

;;;1162   
;;;1163           #if ( configUSE_TRACE_FACILITY == 1 )
;;;1164               {
;;;1165                   /* Add a counter into the TCB for tracing only. */
;;;1166                   pxNewTCB->uxTCBNumber = uxTaskNumber;
;;;1167               }
;;;1168           #endif /* configUSE_TRACE_FACILITY */
;;;1169           traceTASK_CREATE( pxNewTCB );
;;;1170   
;;;1171           prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxNewTCB );
000078  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
00007a  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1              ;1161
00007c  6270              STR      r0,[r6,#0x24]         ;1166  ; uxTaskNumber
00007e  6468              STR      r0,[r5,#0x44]
000080  6ae8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x2c]
000082  6932              LDR      r2,[r6,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000084  4081              LSLS     r1,r1,r0
000086  4311              ORRS     r1,r1,r2
000088  6131              STR      r1,[r6,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00008a  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
00008e  eb070080          ADD      r0,r7,r0,LSL #2
000092  1d29              ADDS     r1,r5,#4
000094  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;1172   
;;;1173           portSETUP_TCB( pxNewTCB );
;;;1174       }
;;;1175       taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000098  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1176   
;;;1177       if( xSchedulerRunning != pdFALSE )
00009c  6970              LDR      r0,[r6,#0x14]  ; xSchedulerRunning
00009e  2800              CMP      r0,#0
0000a0  d00c              BEQ      |L4.188|
;;;1178       {
;;;1179           /* If the created task is of a higher priority than the current task
;;;1180            * then it should run now. */
;;;1181           if( pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority < pxNewTCB->uxPriority )
0000a2  6830              LDR      r0,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0000a4  6ae9              LDR      r1,[r5,#0x2c]
0000a6  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
0000a8  4288              CMP      r0,r1
0000aa  d207              BCS      |L4.188|
;;;1182           {
;;;1183               taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
0000ac  4907              LDR      r1,|L4.204|
0000ae  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
0000b2  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
0000b4  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
0000b8  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L4.188|
;;;1184           }
;;;1185           else
;;;1186           {
;;;1187               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1188           }
;;;1189       }
;;;1190       else
;;;1191       {
;;;1192           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1193       }
;;;1194   }
0000bc  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;1195   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L4.192|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L4.196|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L4.200|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x12c
                  |L4.204|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.prvDeleteTCB||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  prvDeleteTCB PROC
;;;3976   
;;;3977       static void prvDeleteTCB( TCB_t * pxTCB )
000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;3978       {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;3979           /* This call is required specifically for the TriCore port.  It must be
;;;3980            * above the vPortFree() calls.  The call is also used by ports/demos that
;;;3981            * want to allocate and clean RAM statically. */
;;;3982           portCLEAN_UP_TCB( pxTCB );
;;;3983   
;;;3984           /* Free up the memory allocated by the scheduler for the task.  It is up
;;;3985            * to the task to free any memory allocated at the application level.
;;;3986            * See the third party link http://www.nadler.com/embedded/newlibAndFreeRTOS.html
;;;3987            * for additional information. */
;;;3988           #if ( configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT == 1 )
;;;3989               {
;;;3990                   _reclaim_reent( &( pxTCB->xNewLib_reent ) );
;;;3991               }
;;;3992           #endif /* configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT */
;;;3993   
;;;3994           #if ( ( configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION == 1 ) && ( configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION == 0 ) && ( portUSING_MPU_WRAPPERS == 0 ) )
;;;3995               {
;;;3996                   /* The task can only have been allocated dynamically - free both
;;;3997                    * the stack and TCB. */
;;;3998                   vPortFree( pxTCB->pxStack );
000004  6b00              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x30]
000006  f7fffffe          BL       vPortFree
;;;3999                   vPortFree( pxTCB );
00000a  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00000c  e8bd4010          POP      {r4,lr}
000010  f7ffbffe          B.W      vPortFree
;;;4000               }
;;;4001           #elif ( tskSTATIC_AND_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION_POSSIBLE != 0 ) /*lint !e731 !e9029 Macro has been consolidated for readability reasons. */
;;;4002               {
;;;4003                   /* The task could have been allocated statically or dynamically, so
;;;4004                    * check what was statically allocated before trying to free the
;;;4005                    * memory. */
;;;4006                   if( pxTCB->ucStaticallyAllocated == tskDYNAMICALLY_ALLOCATED_STACK_AND_TCB )
;;;4007                   {
;;;4008                       /* Both the stack and TCB were allocated dynamically, so both
;;;4009                        * must be freed. */
;;;4010                       vPortFree( pxTCB->pxStack );
;;;4011                       vPortFree( pxTCB );
;;;4012                   }
;;;4013                   else if( pxTCB->ucStaticallyAllocated == tskSTATICALLY_ALLOCATED_STACK_ONLY )
;;;4014                   {
;;;4015                       /* Only the stack was statically allocated, so the TCB is the
;;;4016                        * only memory that must be freed. */
;;;4017                       vPortFree( pxTCB );
;;;4018                   }
;;;4019                   else
;;;4020                   {
;;;4021                       /* Neither the stack nor the TCB were allocated dynamically, so
;;;4022                        * nothing needs to be freed. */
;;;4023                       configASSERT( pxTCB->ucStaticallyAllocated == tskSTATICALLY_ALLOCATED_STACK_AND_TCB );
;;;4024                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4025                   }
;;;4026               }
;;;4027           #endif /* configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION */
;;;4028       }
;;;4029   
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.prvIdleTask||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  prvIdleTask PROC
;;;3467    */
;;;3468   static portTASK_FUNCTION( prvIdleTask, pvParameters )
000000  4e13              LDR      r6,|L6.80|
000002  4d14              LDR      r5,|L6.84|
;;;3469   {
;;;3470       /* Stop warnings. */
;;;3471       ( void ) pvParameters;
;;;3472   
;;;3473       /** THIS IS THE RTOS IDLE TASK - WHICH IS CREATED AUTOMATICALLY WHEN THE
;;;3474        * SCHEDULER IS STARTED. **/
;;;3475   
;;;3476       /* In case a task that has a secure context deletes itself, in which case
;;;3477        * the idle task is responsible for deleting the task's secure context, if
;;;3478        * any. */
;;;3479       portALLOCATE_SECURE_CONTEXT( configMINIMAL_SECURE_STACK_SIZE );
;;;3480   
;;;3481       for( ; ; )
;;;3482       {
;;;3483           /* See if any tasks have deleted themselves - if so then the idle task
;;;3484            * is responsible for freeing the deleted task's TCB and stack. */
;;;3485           prvCheckTasksWaitingTermination();
;;;3486   
;;;3487           #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 0 )
;;;3488               {
;;;3489                   /* If we are not using preemption we keep forcing a task switch to
;;;3490                    * see if any other task has become available.  If we are using
;;;3491                    * preemption we don't need to do this as any task becoming available
;;;3492                    * will automatically get the processor anyway. */
;;;3493                   taskYIELD();
;;;3494               }
;;;3495           #endif /* configUSE_PREEMPTION */
;;;3496   
;;;3497           #if ( ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 ) && ( configIDLE_SHOULD_YIELD == 1 ) )
;;;3498               {
;;;3499                   /* When using preemption tasks of equal priority will be
;;;3500                    * timesliced.  If a task that is sharing the idle priority is ready
;;;3501                    * to run then the idle task should yield before the end of the
;;;3502                    * timeslice.
;;;3503                    *
;;;3504                    * A critical region is not required here as we are just reading from
;;;3505                    * the list, and an occasional incorrect value will not matter.  If
;;;3506                    * the ready list at the idle priority contains more than one task
;;;3507                    * then a task other than the idle task is ready to execute. */
;;;3508                   if( listCURRENT_LIST_LENGTH( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ tskIDLE_PRIORITY ] ) ) > ( UBaseType_t ) 1 )
000004  f5a677b4          SUB      r7,r6,#0x168
;;;3509                   {
;;;3510                       taskYIELD();
000008  f04f5880          MOV      r8,#0x10000000
00000c  f8df9048          LDR      r9,|L6.88|
000010  e011              B        |L6.54|
                  |L6.18|
000012  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
000016  68f0              LDR      r0,[r6,#0xc]  ; xTasksWaitingTermination
000018  68c4              LDR      r4,[r0,#0xc]
00001a  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00001c  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
000020  68a8              LDR      r0,[r5,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000022  1e40              SUBS     r0,r0,#1
000024  60a8              STR      r0,[r5,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000026  6868              LDR      r0,[r5,#4]  ; uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
000028  1e40              SUBS     r0,r0,#1
00002a  6068              STR      r0,[r5,#4]  ; uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
00002c  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
000030  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000032  f7fffffe          BL       prvDeleteTCB
                  |L6.54|
000036  6868              LDR      r0,[r5,#4]  ; uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
000038  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00003a  d1ea              BNE      |L6.18|
00003c  6838              LDR      r0,[r7,#0]            ;3508  ; pxReadyTasksLists
00003e  2801              CMP      r0,#1                 ;3508
000040  d9f9              BLS      |L6.54|
000042  f8c98000          STR      r8,[r9,#0]
000046  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00004a  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
00004e  e7f2              B        |L6.54|
;;;3511                   }
;;;3512                   else
;;;3513                   {
;;;3514                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;3515                   }
;;;3516               }
;;;3517           #endif /* ( ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 ) && ( configIDLE_SHOULD_YIELD == 1 ) ) */
;;;3518   
;;;3519           #if ( configUSE_IDLE_HOOK == 1 )
;;;3520               {
;;;3521                   extern void vApplicationIdleHook( void );
;;;3522   
;;;3523                   /* Call the user defined function from within the idle task.  This
;;;3524                    * allows the application designer to add background functionality
;;;3525                    * without the overhead of a separate task.
;;;3526                    * NOTE: vApplicationIdleHook() MUST NOT, UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCES,
;;;3527                    * CALL A FUNCTION THAT MIGHT BLOCK. */
;;;3528                   vApplicationIdleHook();
;;;3529               }
;;;3530           #endif /* configUSE_IDLE_HOOK */
;;;3531   
;;;3532           /* This conditional compilation should use inequality to 0, not equality
;;;3533            * to 1.  This is to ensure portSUPPRESS_TICKS_AND_SLEEP() is called when
;;;3534            * user defined low power mode  implementations require
;;;3535            * configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE to be set to a value other than 1. */
;;;3536           #if ( configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE != 0 )
;;;3537               {
;;;3538                   TickType_t xExpectedIdleTime;
;;;3539   
;;;3540                   /* It is not desirable to suspend then resume the scheduler on
;;;3541                    * each iteration of the idle task.  Therefore, a preliminary
;;;3542                    * test of the expected idle time is performed without the
;;;3543                    * scheduler suspended.  The result here is not necessarily
;;;3544                    * valid. */
;;;3545                   xExpectedIdleTime = prvGetExpectedIdleTime();
;;;3546   
;;;3547                   if( xExpectedIdleTime >= configEXPECTED_IDLE_TIME_BEFORE_SLEEP )
;;;3548                   {
;;;3549                       vTaskSuspendAll();
;;;3550                       {
;;;3551                           /* Now the scheduler is suspended, the expected idle
;;;3552                            * time can be sampled again, and this time its value can
;;;3553                            * be used. */
;;;3554                           configASSERT( xNextTaskUnblockTime >= xTickCount );
;;;3555                           xExpectedIdleTime = prvGetExpectedIdleTime();
;;;3556   
;;;3557                           /* Define the following macro to set xExpectedIdleTime to 0
;;;3558                            * if the application does not want
;;;3559                            * portSUPPRESS_TICKS_AND_SLEEP() to be called. */
;;;3560                           configPRE_SUPPRESS_TICKS_AND_SLEEP_PROCESSING( xExpectedIdleTime );
;;;3561   
;;;3562                           if( xExpectedIdleTime >= configEXPECTED_IDLE_TIME_BEFORE_SLEEP )
;;;3563                           {
;;;3564                               traceLOW_POWER_IDLE_BEGIN();
;;;3565                               portSUPPRESS_TICKS_AND_SLEEP( xExpectedIdleTime );
;;;3566                               traceLOW_POWER_IDLE_END();
;;;3567                           }
;;;3568                           else
;;;3569                           {
;;;3570                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;3571                           }
;;;3572                       }
;;;3573                       ( void ) xTaskResumeAll();
;;;3574                   }
;;;3575                   else
;;;3576                   {
;;;3577                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;3578                   }
;;;3579               }
;;;3580           #endif /* configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE */
;;;3581       }
;;;3582   }
;;;3583   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L6.80|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x168
                  |L6.84|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L6.88|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.prvInitialiseNewTask||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  prvInitialiseNewTask PROC
;;;861    
;;;862    static void prvInitialiseNewTask( TaskFunction_t pxTaskCode,
000000  e92d5ff0          PUSH     {r4-r12,lr}
;;;863                                      const char * const pcName, /*lint !e971 Unqualified char types are allowed for strings and single characters only. */
;;;864                                      const uint32_t ulStackDepth,
;;;865                                      void * const pvParameters,
;;;866                                      UBaseType_t uxPriority,
;;;867                                      TaskHandle_t * const pxCreatedTask,
;;;868                                      TCB_t * pxNewTCB,
;;;869                                      const MemoryRegion_t * const xRegions )
;;;870    {
000004  460e              MOV      r6,r1
000006  e9dd940b          LDRD     r9,r4,[sp,#0x2c]
00000a  4617              MOV      r7,r2
00000c  4683              MOV      r11,r0
;;;871        StackType_t * pxTopOfStack;
;;;872        UBaseType_t x;
;;;873    
;;;874        #if ( portUSING_MPU_WRAPPERS == 1 )
;;;875            /* Should the task be created in privileged mode? */
;;;876            BaseType_t xRunPrivileged;
;;;877    
;;;878            if( ( uxPriority & portPRIVILEGE_BIT ) != 0U )
;;;879            {
;;;880                xRunPrivileged = pdTRUE;
;;;881            }
;;;882            else
;;;883            {
;;;884                xRunPrivileged = pdFALSE;
;;;885            }
;;;886            uxPriority &= ~portPRIVILEGE_BIT;
;;;887        #endif /* portUSING_MPU_WRAPPERS == 1 */
;;;888    
;;;889        /* Avoid dependency on memset() if it is not required. */
;;;890        #if ( tskSET_NEW_STACKS_TO_KNOWN_VALUE == 1 )
;;;891            {
;;;892                /* Fill the stack with a known value to assist debugging. */
;;;893                ( void ) memset( pxNewTCB->pxStack, ( int ) tskSTACK_FILL_BYTE, ( size_t ) ulStackDepth * sizeof( StackType_t ) );
00000e  0091              LSLS     r1,r2,#2
000010  469a              MOV      r10,r3                ;870
000012  9d0a              LDR      r5,[sp,#0x28]
000014  22a5              MOVS     r2,#0xa5
000016  6b20              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x30]
000018  f7fffffe          BL       __aeabi_memset
;;;894            }
;;;895        #endif /* tskSET_NEW_STACKS_TO_KNOWN_VALUE */
;;;896    
;;;897        /* Calculate the top of stack address.  This depends on whether the stack
;;;898         * grows from high memory to low (as per the 80x86) or vice versa.
;;;899         * portSTACK_GROWTH is used to make the result positive or negative as required
;;;900         * by the port. */
;;;901        #if ( portSTACK_GROWTH < 0 )
;;;902            {
;;;903                pxTopOfStack = &( pxNewTCB->pxStack[ ulStackDepth - ( uint32_t ) 1 ] );
00001c  f06f0103          MVN      r1,#3
000020  6b20              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x30]
000022  eb010187          ADD      r1,r1,r7,LSL #2
000026  4408              ADD      r0,r0,r1
;;;904                pxTopOfStack = ( StackType_t * ) ( ( ( portPOINTER_SIZE_TYPE ) pxTopOfStack ) & ( ~( ( portPOINTER_SIZE_TYPE ) portBYTE_ALIGNMENT_MASK ) ) ); /*lint !e923 !e9033 !e9078 MISRA exception.  Avoiding casts between pointers and integers is not practical.  Size differences accounted for using portPOINTER_SIZE_TYPE type.  Checked by assert(). */
000028  f0200707          BIC      r7,r0,#7
00002c  f04f0800          MOV      r8,#0
;;;905    
;;;906                /* Check the alignment of the calculated top of stack is correct. */
;;;907                configASSERT( ( ( ( portPOINTER_SIZE_TYPE ) pxTopOfStack & ( portPOINTER_SIZE_TYPE ) portBYTE_ALIGNMENT_MASK ) == 0UL ) );
;;;908    
;;;909                #if ( configRECORD_STACK_HIGH_ADDRESS == 1 )
;;;910                    {
;;;911                        /* Also record the stack's high address, which may assist
;;;912                         * debugging. */
;;;913                        pxNewTCB->pxEndOfStack = pxTopOfStack;
;;;914                    }
;;;915                #endif /* configRECORD_STACK_HIGH_ADDRESS */
;;;916            }
;;;917        #else /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;918            {
;;;919                pxTopOfStack = pxNewTCB->pxStack;
;;;920    
;;;921                /* Check the alignment of the stack buffer is correct. */
;;;922                configASSERT( ( ( ( portPOINTER_SIZE_TYPE ) pxNewTCB->pxStack & ( portPOINTER_SIZE_TYPE ) portBYTE_ALIGNMENT_MASK ) == 0UL ) );
;;;923    
;;;924                /* The other extreme of the stack space is required if stack checking is
;;;925                 * performed. */
;;;926                pxNewTCB->pxEndOfStack = pxNewTCB->pxStack + ( ulStackDepth - ( uint32_t ) 1 );
;;;927            }
;;;928        #endif /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;929    
;;;930        /* Store the task name in the TCB. */
;;;931        if( pcName != NULL )
000030  b166              CBZ      r6,|L7.76|
;;;932        {
;;;933            for( x = ( UBaseType_t ) 0; x < ( UBaseType_t ) configMAX_TASK_NAME_LEN; x++ )
000032  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L7.52|
;;;934            {
;;;935                pxNewTCB->pcTaskName[ x ] = pcName[ x ];
000034  1822              ADDS     r2,r4,r0
000036  5c31              LDRB     r1,[r6,r0]
000038  f8821034          STRB     r1,[r2,#0x34]
;;;936    
;;;937                /* Don't copy all configMAX_TASK_NAME_LEN if the string is shorter than
;;;938                 * configMAX_TASK_NAME_LEN characters just in case the memory after the
;;;939                 * string is not accessible (extremely unlikely). */
;;;940                if( pcName[ x ] == ( char ) 0x00 )
00003c  5c31              LDRB     r1,[r6,r0]
00003e  b111              CBZ      r1,|L7.70|
000040  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000042  2810              CMP      r0,#0x10              ;933
000044  d3f6              BCC      |L7.52|
                  |L7.70|
;;;941                {
;;;942                    break;
;;;943                }
;;;944                else
;;;945                {
;;;946                    mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;947                }
;;;948            }
;;;949    
;;;950            /* Ensure the name string is terminated in the case that the string length
;;;951             * was greater or equal to configMAX_TASK_NAME_LEN. */
;;;952            pxNewTCB->pcTaskName[ configMAX_TASK_NAME_LEN - 1 ] = '\0';
000046  f8848043          STRB     r8,[r4,#0x43]
00004a  e001              B        |L7.80|
                  |L7.76|
;;;953        }
;;;954        else
;;;955        {
;;;956            /* The task has not been given a name, so just ensure there is a NULL
;;;957             * terminator when it is read out. */
;;;958            pxNewTCB->pcTaskName[ 0 ] = 0x00;
00004c  f8848034          STRB     r8,[r4,#0x34]
                  |L7.80|
;;;959        }
;;;960    
;;;961        /* This is used as an array index so must ensure it's not too large.  First
;;;962         * remove the privilege bit if one is present. */
;;;963        if( uxPriority >= ( UBaseType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES )
000050  2d0f              CMP      r5,#0xf
000052  d300              BCC      |L7.86|
;;;964        {
;;;965            uxPriority = ( UBaseType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( UBaseType_t ) 1U;
000054  250e              MOVS     r5,#0xe
                  |L7.86|
;;;966        }
;;;967        else
;;;968        {
;;;969            mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;970        }
;;;971    
;;;972        pxNewTCB->uxPriority = uxPriority;
;;;973        #if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 )
;;;974            {
;;;975                pxNewTCB->uxBasePriority = uxPriority;
;;;976                pxNewTCB->uxMutexesHeld = 0;
;;;977            }
;;;978        #endif /* configUSE_MUTEXES */
;;;979    
;;;980        vListInitialiseItem( &( pxNewTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000056  62e5              STR      r5,[r4,#0x2c]
000058  e9c45813          STRD     r5,r8,[r4,#0x4c]
00005c  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00005e  f7fffffe          BL       vListInitialiseItem
;;;981        vListInitialiseItem( &( pxNewTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000062  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
000066  f7fffffe          BL       vListInitialiseItem
;;;982    
;;;983        /* Set the pxNewTCB as a link back from the ListItem_t.  This is so we can get
;;;984         * back to  the containing TCB from a generic item in a list. */
;;;985        listSET_LIST_ITEM_OWNER( &( pxNewTCB->xStateListItem ), pxNewTCB );
00006a  6124              STR      r4,[r4,#0x10]
;;;986    
;;;987        /* Event lists are always in priority order. */
;;;988        listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxNewTCB->xEventListItem ), ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) uxPriority ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
00006c  f1c5000f          RSB      r0,r5,#0xf
;;;989        listSET_LIST_ITEM_OWNER( &( pxNewTCB->xEventListItem ), pxNewTCB );
000070  6264              STR      r4,[r4,#0x24]
000072  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
;;;990    
;;;991        #if ( portCRITICAL_NESTING_IN_TCB == 1 )
;;;992            {
;;;993                pxNewTCB->uxCriticalNesting = ( UBaseType_t ) 0U;
;;;994            }
;;;995        #endif /* portCRITICAL_NESTING_IN_TCB */
;;;996    
;;;997        #if ( configUSE_APPLICATION_TASK_TAG == 1 )
;;;998            {
;;;999                pxNewTCB->pxTaskTag = NULL;
;;;1000           }
;;;1001       #endif /* configUSE_APPLICATION_TASK_TAG */
;;;1002   
;;;1003       #if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 )
;;;1004           {
;;;1005               pxNewTCB->ulRunTimeCounter = 0UL;
;;;1006           }
;;;1007       #endif /* configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS */
;;;1008   
;;;1009       #if ( portUSING_MPU_WRAPPERS == 1 )
;;;1010           {
;;;1011               vPortStoreTaskMPUSettings( &( pxNewTCB->xMPUSettings ), xRegions, pxNewTCB->pxStack, ulStackDepth );
;;;1012           }
;;;1013       #else
;;;1014           {
;;;1015               /* Avoid compiler warning about unreferenced parameter. */
;;;1016               ( void ) xRegions;
;;;1017           }
;;;1018       #endif
;;;1019   
;;;1020       #if ( configNUM_THREAD_LOCAL_STORAGE_POINTERS != 0 )
;;;1021           {
;;;1022               memset( ( void * ) &( pxNewTCB->pvThreadLocalStoragePointers[ 0 ] ), 0x00, sizeof( pxNewTCB->pvThreadLocalStoragePointers ) );
;;;1023           }
;;;1024       #endif
;;;1025   
;;;1026       #if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 )
;;;1027           {
;;;1028               memset( ( void * ) &( pxNewTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ 0 ] ), 0x00, sizeof( pxNewTCB->ulNotifiedValue ) );
000074  f8c48054          STR      r8,[r4,#0x54]
;;;1029               memset( ( void * ) &( pxNewTCB->ucNotifyState[ 0 ] ), 0x00, sizeof( pxNewTCB->ucNotifyState ) );
000078  f8c48058          STR      r8,[r4,#0x58]
00007c  f884805c          STRB     r8,[r4,#0x5c]
;;;1030           }
;;;1031       #endif
;;;1032   
;;;1033       #if ( configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT == 1 )
;;;1034           {
;;;1035               /* Initialise this task's Newlib reent structure.
;;;1036                * See the third party link http://www.nadler.com/embedded/newlibAndFreeRTOS.html
;;;1037                * for additional information. */
;;;1038               _REENT_INIT_PTR( ( &( pxNewTCB->xNewLib_reent ) ) );
;;;1039           }
;;;1040       #endif
;;;1041   
;;;1042       #if ( INCLUDE_xTaskAbortDelay == 1 )
;;;1043           {
;;;1044               pxNewTCB->ucDelayAborted = pdFALSE;
;;;1045           }
;;;1046       #endif
;;;1047   
;;;1048       /* Initialize the TCB stack to look as if the task was already running,
;;;1049        * but had been interrupted by the scheduler.  The return address is set
;;;1050        * to the start of the task function. Once the stack has been initialised
;;;1051        * the top of stack variable is updated. */
;;;1052       #if ( portUSING_MPU_WRAPPERS == 1 )
;;;1053           {
;;;1054               /* If the port has capability to detect stack overflow,
;;;1055                * pass the stack end address to the stack initialization
;;;1056                * function as well. */
;;;1057               #if ( portHAS_STACK_OVERFLOW_CHECKING == 1 )
;;;1058                   {
;;;1059                       #if ( portSTACK_GROWTH < 0 )
;;;1060                           {
;;;1061                               pxNewTCB->pxTopOfStack = pxPortInitialiseStack( pxTopOfStack, pxNewTCB->pxStack, pxTaskCode, pvParameters, xRunPrivileged );
;;;1062                           }
;;;1063                       #else /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;1064                           {
;;;1065                               pxNewTCB->pxTopOfStack = pxPortInitialiseStack( pxTopOfStack, pxNewTCB->pxEndOfStack, pxTaskCode, pvParameters, xRunPrivileged );
;;;1066                           }
;;;1067                       #endif /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;1068                   }
;;;1069               #else /* portHAS_STACK_OVERFLOW_CHECKING */
;;;1070                   {
;;;1071                       pxNewTCB->pxTopOfStack = pxPortInitialiseStack( pxTopOfStack, pxTaskCode, pvParameters, xRunPrivileged );
;;;1072                   }
;;;1073               #endif /* portHAS_STACK_OVERFLOW_CHECKING */
;;;1074           }
;;;1075       #else /* portUSING_MPU_WRAPPERS */
;;;1076           {
;;;1077               /* If the port has capability to detect stack overflow,
;;;1078                * pass the stack end address to the stack initialization
;;;1079                * function as well. */
;;;1080               #if ( portHAS_STACK_OVERFLOW_CHECKING == 1 )
;;;1081                   {
;;;1082                       #if ( portSTACK_GROWTH < 0 )
;;;1083                           {
;;;1084                               pxNewTCB->pxTopOfStack = pxPortInitialiseStack( pxTopOfStack, pxNewTCB->pxStack, pxTaskCode, pvParameters );
;;;1085                           }
;;;1086                       #else /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;1087                           {
;;;1088                               pxNewTCB->pxTopOfStack = pxPortInitialiseStack( pxTopOfStack, pxNewTCB->pxEndOfStack, pxTaskCode, pvParameters );
;;;1089                           }
;;;1090                       #endif /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;1091                   }
;;;1092               #else /* portHAS_STACK_OVERFLOW_CHECKING */
;;;1093                   {
;;;1094                       pxNewTCB->pxTopOfStack = pxPortInitialiseStack( pxTopOfStack, pxTaskCode, pvParameters );
000080  4652              MOV      r2,r10
000082  4659              MOV      r1,r11
000084  4638              MOV      r0,r7
000086  f7fffffe          BL       pxPortInitialiseStack
;;;1095                   }
;;;1096               #endif /* portHAS_STACK_OVERFLOW_CHECKING */
;;;1097           }
;;;1098       #endif /* portUSING_MPU_WRAPPERS */
;;;1099   
;;;1100       if( pxCreatedTask != NULL )
00008a  6020              STR      r0,[r4,#0]
00008c  f1b90f00          CMP      r9,#0
000090  d001              BEQ      |L7.150|
;;;1101       {
;;;1102           /* Pass the handle out in an anonymous way.  The handle can be used to
;;;1103            * change the created task's priority, delete the created task, etc.*/
;;;1104           *pxCreatedTask = ( TaskHandle_t ) pxNewTCB;
000092  f8c94000          STR      r4,[r9,#0]
                  |L7.150|
;;;1105       }
;;;1106       else
;;;1107       {
;;;1108           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1109       }
;;;1110   }
000096  e8bd9ff0          POP      {r4-r12,pc}
;;;1111   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.prvListTasksWithinSingleList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  prvListTasksWithinSingleList PROC
;;;3853   
;;;3854       static UBaseType_t prvListTasksWithinSingleList( TaskStatus_t * pxTaskStatusArray,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;3855                                                        List_t * pxList,
;;;3856                                                        eTaskState eState )
;;;3857       {
000004  4681              MOV      r9,r0
;;;3858           configLIST_VOLATILE TCB_t * pxNextTCB, * pxFirstTCB;
;;;3859           UBaseType_t uxTask = 0;
;;;3860   
;;;3861           if( listCURRENT_LIST_LENGTH( pxList ) > ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000006  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]
000008  4692              MOV      r10,r2                ;3857
00000a  460c              MOV      r4,r1                 ;3857
00000c  2500              MOVS     r5,#0                 ;3859
00000e  b1f8              CBZ      r0,|L8.80|
;;;3862           {
;;;3863               listGET_OWNER_OF_NEXT_ENTRY( pxFirstTCB, pxList ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000010  6848              LDR      r0,[r1,#4]
000012  f1010608          ADD      r6,r1,#8
000016  6840              LDR      r0,[r0,#4]
000018  6048              STR      r0,[r1,#4]
00001a  42b0              CMP      r0,r6
00001c  d101              BNE      |L8.34|
00001e  6840              LDR      r0,[r0,#4]
000020  6048              STR      r0,[r1,#4]
                  |L8.34|
000022  f8d0800c          LDR      r8,[r0,#0xc]
                  |L8.38|
;;;3864   
;;;3865               /* Populate an TaskStatus_t structure within the
;;;3866                * pxTaskStatusArray array for each task that is referenced from
;;;3867                * pxList.  See the definition of TaskStatus_t in task.h for the
;;;3868                * meaning of each TaskStatus_t structure member. */
;;;3869               do
;;;3870               {
;;;3871                   listGET_OWNER_OF_NEXT_ENTRY( pxNextTCB, pxList ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000026  6860              LDR      r0,[r4,#4]
000028  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
00002a  6062              STR      r2,[r4,#4]
00002c  42b2              CMP      r2,r6
00002e  d101              BNE      |L8.52|
000030  6851              LDR      r1,[r2,#4]
000032  6061              STR      r1,[r4,#4]
                  |L8.52|
000034  6860              LDR      r0,[r4,#4]
;;;3872                   vTaskGetInfo( ( TaskHandle_t ) pxNextTCB, &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), pdTRUE, eState );
000036  eb0501c5          ADD      r1,r5,r5,LSL #3
00003a  eb090181          ADD      r1,r9,r1,LSL #2
00003e  68c7              LDR      r7,[r0,#0xc]
000040  4653              MOV      r3,r10
000042  2201              MOVS     r2,#1
000044  4638              MOV      r0,r7
000046  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskGetInfo
00004a  1c6d              ADDS     r5,r5,#1
;;;3873                   uxTask++;
;;;3874               } while( pxNextTCB != pxFirstTCB );
00004c  4547              CMP      r7,r8
00004e  d1ea              BNE      |L8.38|
                  |L8.80|
;;;3875           }
;;;3876           else
;;;3877           {
;;;3878               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;3879           }
;;;3880   
;;;3881           return uxTask;
000050  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;3882       }
000052  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;3883   
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime PROC
;;;4032   
;;;4033   static void prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime( void )
000000  4805              LDR      r0,|L9.24|
;;;4034   {
;;;4035       if( listLIST_IS_EMPTY( pxDelayedTaskList ) != pdFALSE )
000002  6c01              LDR      r1,[r0,#0x40]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000004  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]
000006  b121              CBZ      r1,|L9.18|
;;;4036       {
;;;4037           /* The new current delayed list is empty.  Set xNextTaskUnblockTime to
;;;4038            * the maximum possible value so it is  extremely unlikely that the
;;;4039            * if( xTickCount >= xNextTaskUnblockTime ) test will pass until
;;;4040            * there is an item in the delayed list. */
;;;4041           xNextTaskUnblockTime = portMAX_DELAY;
;;;4042       }
;;;4043       else
;;;4044       {
;;;4045           /* The new current delayed list is not empty, get the value of
;;;4046            * the item at the head of the delayed list.  This is the time at
;;;4047            * which the task at the head of the delayed list should be removed
;;;4048            * from the Blocked state. */
;;;4049           xNextTaskUnblockTime = listGET_ITEM_VALUE_OF_HEAD_ENTRY( pxDelayedTaskList );
000008  6c01              LDR      r1,[r0,#0x40]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
00000a  68c9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0xc]
00000c  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]
                  |L9.14|
00000e  6281              STR      r1,[r0,#0x28]         ;4041  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;4050       }
;;;4051   }
000010  4770              BX       lr
                  |L9.18|
000012  f04f31ff          MOV      r1,#0xffffffff        ;4041
000016  e7fa              B        |L9.14|
;;;4052   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L9.24|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.prvTaskIsTaskSuspended||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  prvTaskIsTaskSuspended PROC
;;;1856   
;;;1857       static BaseType_t prvTaskIsTaskSuspended( const TaskHandle_t xTask )
000000  4b06              LDR      r3,|L10.28|
;;;1858       {
;;;1859           BaseType_t xReturn = pdFALSE;
;;;1860           const TCB_t * const pxTCB = xTask;
;;;1861   
;;;1862           /* Accesses xPendingReadyList so must be called from a critical
;;;1863            * section. */
;;;1864   
;;;1865           /* It does not make sense to check if the calling task is suspended. */
;;;1866           configASSERT( xTask );
;;;1867   
;;;1868           /* Is the task being resumed actually in the suspended list? */
;;;1869           if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( &xSuspendedTaskList, &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) != pdFALSE )
000002  6942              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x14]
000004  2100              MOVS     r1,#0                 ;1859
000006  429a              CMP      r2,r3
000008  d106              BNE      |L10.24|
;;;1870           {
;;;1871               /* Has the task already been resumed from within an ISR? */
;;;1872               if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( &xPendingReadyList, &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == pdFALSE )
00000a  6a80              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x28]
00000c  f1a30228          SUB      r2,r3,#0x28
000010  4290              CMP      r0,r2
000012  d001              BEQ      |L10.24|
;;;1873               {
;;;1874                   /* Is it in the suspended list because it is in the Suspended
;;;1875                    * state, or because is is blocked with no timeout? */
;;;1876                   if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( NULL, &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != pdFALSE ) /*lint !e961.  The cast is only redundant when NULL is used. */
000014  b900              CBNZ     r0,|L10.24|
;;;1877                   {
;;;1878                       xReturn = pdTRUE;
000016  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
                  |L10.24|
;;;1879                   }
;;;1880                   else
;;;1881                   {
;;;1882                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1883                   }
;;;1884               }
;;;1885               else
;;;1886               {
;;;1887                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1888               }
;;;1889           }
;;;1890           else
;;;1891           {
;;;1892               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1893           }
;;;1894   
;;;1895           return xReturn;
000018  4608              MOV      r0,r1
;;;1896       } /*lint !e818 xTask cannot be a pointer to const because it is a typedef. */
00001a  4770              BX       lr
;;;1897   
                          ENDP

                  |L10.28|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x17c

                          AREA ||i.prvWriteNameToBuffer||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  prvWriteNameToBuffer PROC
;;;4439   
;;;4440       static char * prvWriteNameToBuffer( char * pcBuffer,
000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;4441                                           const char * pcTaskName )
;;;4442       {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;4443           size_t x;
;;;4444   
;;;4445           /* Start by copying the entire string. */
;;;4446           strcpy( pcBuffer, pcTaskName );
000004  f7fffffe          BL       strcpy
;;;4447   
;;;4448           /* Pad the end of the string with spaces to ensure columns line up when
;;;4449            * printed out. */
;;;4450           for( x = strlen( pcBuffer ); x < ( size_t ) ( configMAX_TASK_NAME_LEN - 1 ); x++ )
000008  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00000a  f7fffffe          BL       strlen
;;;4451           {
;;;4452               pcBuffer[ x ] = ' ';
00000e  2120              MOVS     r1,#0x20
000010  e001              B        |L11.22|
                  |L11.18|
000012  5421              STRB     r1,[r4,r0]
000014  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
                  |L11.22|
000016  280f              CMP      r0,#0xf               ;4450
000018  d3fb              BCC      |L11.18|
;;;4453           }
;;;4454   
;;;4455           /* Terminate. */
;;;4456           pcBuffer[ x ] = ( char ) 0x00;
00001a  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
00001c  5421              STRB     r1,[r4,r0]
;;;4457   
;;;4458           /* Return the new end of string. */
;;;4459           return &( pcBuffer[ x ] );
00001e  4420              ADD      r0,r0,r4
;;;4460       }
000020  bd10              POP      {r4,pc}
;;;4461   
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.pvTaskIncrementMutexHeldCount||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  pvTaskIncrementMutexHeldCount PROC
;;;4711   
;;;4712       TaskHandle_t pvTaskIncrementMutexHeldCount( void )
000000  4904              LDR      r1,|L12.20|
;;;4713       {
;;;4714           /* If xSemaphoreCreateMutex() is called before any tasks have been created
;;;4715            * then pxCurrentTCB will be NULL. */
;;;4716           if( pxCurrentTCB != NULL )
000002  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000004  b118              CBZ      r0,|L12.14|
;;;4717           {
;;;4718               ( pxCurrentTCB->uxMutexesHeld )++;
000006  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000008  6d02              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x50]
00000a  1c52              ADDS     r2,r2,#1
00000c  6502              STR      r2,[r0,#0x50]
                  |L12.14|
;;;4719           }
;;;4720   
;;;4721           return pxCurrentTCB;
00000e  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
;;;4722       }
000010  4770              BX       lr
;;;4723   
                          ENDP

000012  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L12.20|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.ulTaskGenericNotifyTake||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  ulTaskGenericNotifyTake PROC
;;;4728   
;;;4729       uint32_t ulTaskGenericNotifyTake( UBaseType_t uxIndexToWait,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;4730                                         BaseType_t xClearCountOnExit,
;;;4731                                         TickType_t xTicksToWait )
;;;4732       {
000004  4615              MOV      r5,r2
000006  460f              MOV      r7,r1
000008  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;4733           uint32_t ulReturn;
;;;4734   
;;;4735           configASSERT( uxIndexToWait < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;4736   
;;;4737           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
00000a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;4738           {
;;;4739               /* Only block if the notification count is not already non-zero. */
;;;4740               if( pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ] == 0UL )
00000e  4e1b              LDR      r6,|L13.124|
000010  6830              LDR      r0,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000012  eb000084          ADD      r0,r0,r4,LSL #2
000016  6d80              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x58]
000018  b988              CBNZ     r0,|L13.62|
;;;4741               {
;;;4742                   /* Mark this task as waiting for a notification. */
;;;4743                   pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] = taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION;
00001a  6831              LDR      r1,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00001c  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00001e  4421              ADD      r1,r1,r4
000020  f881005c          STRB     r0,[r1,#0x5c]
;;;4744   
;;;4745                   if( xTicksToWait > ( TickType_t ) 0 )
000024  b15d              CBZ      r5,|L13.62|
;;;4746                   {
;;;4747                       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToWait, pdTRUE );
000026  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000028  4628              MOV      r0,r5
00002a  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;4748                       traceTASK_NOTIFY_TAKE_BLOCK( uxIndexToWait );
;;;4749   
;;;4750                       /* All ports are written to allow a yield in a critical
;;;4751                        * section (some will yield immediately, others wait until the
;;;4752                        * critical section exits) - but it is not something that
;;;4753                        * application code should ever do. */
;;;4754                       portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
00002e  4914              LDR      r1,|L13.128|
000030  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000034  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000036  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00003a  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L13.62|
;;;4755                   }
;;;4756                   else
;;;4757                   {
;;;4758                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4759                   }
;;;4760               }
;;;4761               else
;;;4762               {
;;;4763                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4764               }
;;;4765           }
;;;4766           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00003e  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;4767   
;;;4768           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000042  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;4769           {
;;;4770               traceTASK_NOTIFY_TAKE( uxIndexToWait );
;;;4771               ulReturn = pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ];
000046  6830              LDR      r0,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000048  eb000084          ADD      r0,r0,r4,LSL #2
00004c  6d85              LDR      r5,[r0,#0x58]
00004e  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
;;;4772   
;;;4773               if( ulReturn != 0UL )
000050  b155              CBZ      r5,|L13.104|
;;;4774               {
;;;4775                   if( xClearCountOnExit != pdFALSE )
000052  b127              CBZ      r7,|L13.94|
;;;4776                   {
;;;4777                       pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ] = 0UL;
000054  6831              LDR      r1,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000056  eb010184          ADD      r1,r1,r4,LSL #2
00005a  6588              STR      r0,[r1,#0x58]
00005c  e004              B        |L13.104|
                  |L13.94|
;;;4778                   }
;;;4779                   else
;;;4780                   {
;;;4781                       pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ] = ulReturn - ( uint32_t ) 1;
00005e  6832              LDR      r2,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000060  1e69              SUBS     r1,r5,#1
000062  eb020284          ADD      r2,r2,r4,LSL #2
000066  6591              STR      r1,[r2,#0x58]
                  |L13.104|
;;;4782                   }
;;;4783               }
;;;4784               else
;;;4785               {
;;;4786                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4787               }
;;;4788   
;;;4789               pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] = taskNOT_WAITING_NOTIFICATION;
000068  6831              LDR      r1,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00006a  4421              ADD      r1,r1,r4
00006c  f881005c          STRB     r0,[r1,#0x5c]
;;;4790           }
;;;4791           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000070  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;4792   
;;;4793           return ulReturn;
000074  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;4794       }
000076  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;4795   
                          ENDP

00007a  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L13.124|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L13.128|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.ulTaskGenericNotifyValueClear||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  ulTaskGenericNotifyValueClear PROC
;;;5271   
;;;5272       uint32_t ulTaskGenericNotifyValueClear( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;5273                                               UBaseType_t uxIndexToClear,
;;;5274                                               uint32_t ulBitsToClear )
;;;5275       {
000002  4615              MOV      r5,r2
000004  460e              MOV      r6,r1
000006  b160              CBZ      r0,|L14.34|
;;;5276           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;5277           uint32_t ulReturn;
;;;5278   
;;;5279           /* If null is passed in here then it is the calling task that is having
;;;5280            * its notification state cleared. */
;;;5281           pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000008  4604              MOV      r4,r0
                  |L14.10|
;;;5282   
;;;5283           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
00000a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;5284           {
;;;5285               /* Return the notification as it was before the bits were cleared,
;;;5286                * then clear the bit mask. */
;;;5287               ulReturn = pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToClear ];
00000e  eb040186          ADD      r1,r4,r6,LSL #2
000012  6d8c              LDR      r4,[r1,#0x58]
;;;5288               pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToClear ] &= ~ulBitsToClear;
000014  6d88              LDR      r0,[r1,#0x58]
000016  43a8              BICS     r0,r0,r5
000018  6588              STR      r0,[r1,#0x58]
;;;5289           }
;;;5290           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00001a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;5291   
;;;5292           return ulReturn;
00001e  4620              MOV      r0,r4
;;;5293       }
000020  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
                  |L14.34|
000022  4801              LDR      r0,|L14.40|
000024  6804              LDR      r4,[r0,#0]            ;5281  ; pxCurrentTCB
000026  e7f0              B        |L14.10|
;;;5294   
                          ENDP

                  |L14.40|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks PROC
;;;2411   
;;;2412   UBaseType_t uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks( void )
000000  4801              LDR      r0,|L15.8|
;;;2413   {
;;;2414       /* A critical section is not required because the variables are of type
;;;2415        * BaseType_t. */
;;;2416       return uxCurrentNumberOfTasks;
000002  6880              LDR      r0,[r0,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
;;;2417   }
000004  4770              BX       lr
;;;2418   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000006  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L15.8|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskGetSystemState||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskGetSystemState PROC
;;;2566   
;;;2567       UBaseType_t uxTaskGetSystemState( TaskStatus_t * const pxTaskStatusArray,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;2568                                         const UBaseType_t uxArraySize,
;;;2569                                         uint32_t * const pulTotalRunTime )
;;;2570       {
000004  4617              MOV      r7,r2
000006  460a              MOV      r2,r1
000008  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;2571           UBaseType_t uxTask = 0, uxQueue = configMAX_PRIORITIES;
00000a  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
00000c  250f              MOVS     r5,#0xf
;;;2572   
;;;2573           vTaskSuspendAll();
00000e  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;2574           {
;;;2575               /* Is there a space in the array for each task in the system? */
;;;2576               if( uxArraySize >= uxCurrentNumberOfTasks )
000012  f8df808c          LDR      r8,|L16.160|
000016  f8d80008          LDR      r0,[r8,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
00001a  4282              CMP      r2,r0
00001c  d33b              BCC      |L16.150|
;;;2577               {
;;;2578                   /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on each
;;;2579                    * task in the Ready state. */
;;;2580                   do
;;;2581                   {
;;;2582                       uxQueue--;
;;;2583                       uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), &( pxReadyTasksLists[ uxQueue ] ), eReady );
00001e  f8df9084          LDR      r9,|L16.164|
                  |L16.34|
000022  1e6d              SUBS     r5,r5,#1
000024  eb050185          ADD      r1,r5,r5,LSL #2
000028  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
00002c  eb090181          ADD      r1,r9,r1,LSL #2
000030  eb060080          ADD      r0,r6,r0,LSL #2
000034  2201              MOVS     r2,#1
000036  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
00003a  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2584                   } while( uxQueue > ( UBaseType_t ) tskIDLE_PRIORITY ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
00003c  2d00              CMP      r5,#0
00003e  d1f0              BNE      |L16.34|
;;;2585   
;;;2586                   /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on each
;;;2587                    * task in the Blocked state. */
;;;2588                   uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), ( List_t * ) pxDelayedTaskList, eBlocked );
000040  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000044  eb060080          ADD      r0,r6,r0,LSL #2
000048  2202              MOVS     r2,#2
00004a  4645              MOV      r5,r8
00004c  f8d81040          LDR      r1,[r8,#0x40]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000050  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
000054  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2589                   uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), ( List_t * ) pxOverflowDelayedTaskList, eBlocked );
000056  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
00005a  eb060080          ADD      r0,r6,r0,LSL #2
00005e  2202              MOVS     r2,#2
000060  6c69              LDR      r1,[r5,#0x44]  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
000062  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
000066  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2590   
;;;2591                   #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskDelete == 1 )
;;;2592                       {
;;;2593                           /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on
;;;2594                            * each task that has been deleted but not yet cleaned up. */
;;;2595                           uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), &xTasksWaitingTermination, eDeleted );
000068  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
00006c  eb060080          ADD      r0,r6,r0,LSL #2
000070  2204              MOVS     r2,#4
000072  490d              LDR      r1,|L16.168|
000074  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
000078  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2596                       }
;;;2597                   #endif
;;;2598   
;;;2599                   #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;2600                       {
;;;2601                           /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on
;;;2602                            * each task in the Suspended state. */
;;;2603                           uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), &xSuspendedTaskList, eSuspended );
00007a  490b              LDR      r1,|L16.168|
00007c  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000080  eb060080          ADD      r0,r6,r0,LSL #2
000084  2203              MOVS     r2,#3
000086  3114              ADDS     r1,r1,#0x14
000088  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
00008c  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2604                       }
;;;2605                   #endif
;;;2606   
;;;2607                   #if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 )
;;;2608                       {
;;;2609                           if( pulTotalRunTime != NULL )
00008e  b117              CBZ      r7,|L16.150|
;;;2610                           {
;;;2611                               #ifdef portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE
;;;2612                                   portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE( ( *pulTotalRunTime ) );
;;;2613                               #else
;;;2614                                   *pulTotalRunTime = portGET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE();
000090  4806              LDR      r0,|L16.172|
000092  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
000094  6038              STR      r0,[r7,#0]
                  |L16.150|
;;;2615                               #endif
;;;2616                           }
;;;2617                       }
;;;2618                   #else /* if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 ) */
;;;2619                       {
;;;2620                           if( pulTotalRunTime != NULL )
;;;2621                           {
;;;2622                               *pulTotalRunTime = 0;
;;;2623                           }
;;;2624                       }
;;;2625                   #endif /* if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 ) */
;;;2626               }
;;;2627               else
;;;2628               {
;;;2629                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2630               }
;;;2631           }
;;;2632           ( void ) xTaskResumeAll();
000096  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
;;;2633   
;;;2634           return uxTask;
00009a  4620              MOV      r0,r4
;;;2635       }
00009c  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;2636   
                          ENDP

                  |L16.160|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L16.164|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L16.168|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x168
                  |L16.172|
                          DCD      ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskGetTaskNumber||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  uxTaskGetTaskNumber PROC
;;;3419   
;;;3420       UBaseType_t uxTaskGetTaskNumber( TaskHandle_t xTask )
000000  2800              CMP      r0,#0
;;;3421       {
000002  d000              BEQ      |L17.6|
;;;3422           UBaseType_t uxReturn;
;;;3423           TCB_t const * pxTCB;
;;;3424   
;;;3425           if( xTask != NULL )
;;;3426           {
;;;3427               pxTCB = xTask;
;;;3428               uxReturn = pxTCB->uxTaskNumber;
000004  6c80              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x48]
                  |L17.6|
;;;3429           }
;;;3430           else
;;;3431           {
;;;3432               uxReturn = 0U;
;;;3433           }
;;;3434   
;;;3435           return uxReturn;
;;;3436       }
000006  4770              BX       lr
;;;3437   
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.uxTaskPriorityGet||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskPriorityGet PROC
;;;1522   
;;;1523       UBaseType_t uxTaskPriorityGet( const TaskHandle_t xTask )
000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;1524       {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1525           TCB_t const * pxTCB;
;;;1526           UBaseType_t uxReturn;
;;;1527   
;;;1528           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1529           {
;;;1530               /* If null is passed in here then it is the priority of the task
;;;1531                * that called uxTaskPriorityGet() that is being queried. */
;;;1532               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000008  b90c              CBNZ     r4,|L18.14|
00000a  4803              LDR      r0,|L18.24|
00000c  6804              LDR      r4,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
                  |L18.14|
;;;1533               uxReturn = pxTCB->uxPriority;
00000e  6ae4              LDR      r4,[r4,#0x2c]
;;;1534           }
;;;1535           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000010  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1536   
;;;1537           return uxReturn;
000014  4620              MOV      r0,r4
;;;1538       }
000016  bd10              POP      {r4,pc}
;;;1539   
                          ENDP

                  |L18.24|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskPriorityGetFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskPriorityGetFromISR PROC
;;;1544   
;;;1545       UBaseType_t uxTaskPriorityGetFromISR( const TaskHandle_t xTask )
000000  22bf              MOVS     r2,#0xbf
000002  f3ef8111          MRS      r1,BASEPRI
000006  f3828811          MSR      BASEPRI,r2
00000a  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00000e  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
;;;1546       {
000012  b908              CBNZ     r0,|L19.24|
;;;1547           TCB_t const * pxTCB;
;;;1548           UBaseType_t uxReturn, uxSavedInterruptState;
;;;1549   
;;;1550           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;1551            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;1552            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;1553            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;1554            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;1555            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;1556            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;1557            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;1558            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;1559            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;1560            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;1561            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;1562            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;1563            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;1564            * provided on the following link:
;;;1565            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;1566           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;1567   
;;;1568           uxSavedInterruptState = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
;;;1569           {
;;;1570               /* If null is passed in here then it is the priority of the calling
;;;1571                * task that is being queried. */
;;;1572               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000014  4802              LDR      r0,|L19.32|
000016  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
                  |L19.24|
;;;1573               uxReturn = pxTCB->uxPriority;
000018  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
00001a  f3818811          MSR      BASEPRI,r1
;;;1574           }
;;;1575           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptState );
;;;1576   
;;;1577           return uxReturn;
;;;1578       }
00001e  4770              BX       lr
;;;1579   
                          ENDP

                  |L19.32|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskResetEventItemValue||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskResetEventItemValue PROC
;;;4695   
;;;4696   TickType_t uxTaskResetEventItemValue( void )
000000  4904              LDR      r1,|L20.20|
;;;4697   {
;;;4698       TickType_t uxReturn;
;;;4699   
;;;4700       uxReturn = listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000002  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
;;;4701   
;;;4702       /* Reset the event list item to its normal value - so it can be used with
;;;4703        * queues and semaphores. */
;;;4704       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ), ( ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority ) ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000004  680a              LDR      r2,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000006  6980              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x18]         ;4700
000008  6ad2              LDR      r2,[r2,#0x2c]
00000a  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000c  f1c2020f          RSB      r2,r2,#0xf
000010  618a              STR      r2,[r1,#0x18]
;;;4705   
;;;4706       return uxReturn;
;;;4707   }
000012  4770              BX       lr
;;;4708   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L20.20|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskDelay||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskDelay PROC
;;;1380   
;;;1381       void vTaskDelay( const TickType_t xTicksToDelay )
