000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;1382       {
000002  0002              MOVS     r2,r0
000004  d009              BEQ      |L21.26|
;;;1383           BaseType_t xAlreadyYielded = pdFALSE;
;;;1384   
;;;1385           /* A delay time of zero just forces a reschedule. */
;;;1386           if( xTicksToDelay > ( TickType_t ) 0U )
;;;1387           {
;;;1388               configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1389               vTaskSuspendAll();
000006  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;1390               {
;;;1391                   traceTASK_DELAY();
;;;1392   
;;;1393                   /* A task that is removed from the event list while the
;;;1394                    * scheduler is suspended will not get placed in the ready
;;;1395                    * list or removed from the blocked list until the scheduler
;;;1396                    * is resumed.
;;;1397                    *
;;;1398                    * This task cannot be in an event list as it is the currently
;;;1399                    * executing task. */
;;;1400                   prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToDelay, pdFALSE );
00000a  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
00000c  4610              MOV      r0,r2
00000e  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;1401               }
;;;1402               xAlreadyYielded = xTaskResumeAll();
000012  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
000016  2800              CMP      r0,#0
000018  d107              BNE      |L21.42|
                  |L21.26|
;;;1403           }
;;;1404           else
;;;1405           {
;;;1406               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1407           }
;;;1408   
;;;1409           /* Force a reschedule if xTaskResumeAll has not already done so, we may
;;;1410            * have put ourselves to sleep. */
;;;1411           if( xAlreadyYielded == pdFALSE )
;;;1412           {
;;;1413               portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
00001a  4904              LDR      r1,|L21.44|
00001c  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000020  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000022  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000026  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L21.42|
;;;1414           }
;;;1415           else
;;;1416           {
;;;1417               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1418           }
;;;1419       }
00002a  bd10              POP      {r4,pc}
;;;1420   
                          ENDP

                  |L21.44|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskDelete||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskDelete PROC
;;;1198   
;;;1199       void vTaskDelete( TaskHandle_t xTaskToDelete )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1200       {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1201           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;1202   
;;;1203           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1204           {
;;;1205               /* If null is passed in here then it is the calling task that is
;;;1206                * being deleted. */
;;;1207               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTaskToDelete );
000008  4d1f              LDR      r5,|L22.136|
00000a  b904              CBNZ     r4,|L22.14|
00000c  682c              LDR      r4,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
                  |L22.14|
;;;1208   
;;;1209               /* Remove task from the ready/delayed list. */
;;;1210               if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
00000e  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000010  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
000014  b958              CBNZ     r0,|L22.46|
;;;1211               {
;;;1212                   taskRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxTCB->uxPriority );
000016  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000018  4a1c              LDR      r2,|L22.140|
00001a  eb000180          ADD      r1,r0,r0,LSL #2
00001e  f8521021          LDR      r1,[r2,r1,LSL #2]
000022  b921              CBNZ     r1,|L22.46|
000024  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000026  4081              LSLS     r1,r1,r0
000028  6928              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00002a  4388              BICS     r0,r0,r1
00002c  6128              STR      r0,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L22.46|
;;;1213               }
;;;1214               else
;;;1215               {
;;;1216                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1217               }
;;;1218   
;;;1219               /* Is the task waiting on an event also? */
;;;1220               if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
00002e  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
000030  b118              CBZ      r0,|L22.58|
;;;1221               {
;;;1222                   ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000032  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
000036  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
                  |L22.58|
;;;1223               }
;;;1224               else
;;;1225               {
;;;1226                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1227               }
;;;1228   
;;;1229               /* Increment the uxTaskNumber also so kernel aware debuggers can
;;;1230                * detect that the task lists need re-generating.  This is done before
;;;1231                * portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK() as in the Windows port that macro will
;;;1232                * not return. */
;;;1233               uxTaskNumber++;
00003a  6a68              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x24]  ; uxTaskNumber
00003c  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
;;;1234   
;;;1235               if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
00003e  6268              STR      r0,[r5,#0x24]  ; uxTaskNumber
000040  6828              LDR      r0,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000042  4284              CMP      r4,r0
000044  d107              BNE      |L22.86|
;;;1236               {
;;;1237                   /* A task is deleting itself.  This cannot complete within the
;;;1238                    * task itself, as a context switch to another task is required.
;;;1239                    * Place the task in the termination list.  The idle task will
;;;1240                    * check the termination list and free up any memory allocated by
;;;1241                    * the scheduler for the TCB and stack of the deleted task. */
;;;1242                   vListInsertEnd( &xTasksWaitingTermination, &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000046  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000048  4811              LDR      r0,|L22.144|
00004a  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;1243   
;;;1244                   /* Increment the ucTasksDeleted variable so the idle task knows
;;;1245                    * there is a task that has been deleted and that it should therefore
;;;1246                    * check the xTasksWaitingTermination list. */
;;;1247                   ++uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp;
00004e  6868              LDR      r0,[r5,#4]  ; uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
000050  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000052  6068              STR      r0,[r5,#4]  ; uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
000054  e007              B        |L22.102|
                  |L22.86|
;;;1248   
;;;1249                   /* Call the delete hook before portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK() as
;;;1250                    * portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK() does not return in the Win32 port. */
;;;1251                   traceTASK_DELETE( pxTCB );
;;;1252   
;;;1253                   /* The pre-delete hook is primarily for the Windows simulator,
;;;1254                    * in which Windows specific clean up operations are performed,
;;;1255                    * after which it is not possible to yield away from this task -
;;;1256                    * hence xYieldPending is used to latch that a context switch is
;;;1257                    * required. */
;;;1258                   portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK( pxTCB, &xYieldPending );
;;;1259               }
;;;1260               else
;;;1261               {
;;;1262                   --uxCurrentNumberOfTasks;
000056  68a8              LDR      r0,[r5,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000058  1e40              SUBS     r0,r0,#1
00005a  60a8              STR      r0,[r5,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
;;;1263                   traceTASK_DELETE( pxTCB );
;;;1264                   prvDeleteTCB( pxTCB );
00005c  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00005e  f7fffffe          BL       prvDeleteTCB
;;;1265   
;;;1266                   /* Reset the next expected unblock time in case it referred to
;;;1267                    * the task that has just been deleted. */
;;;1268                   prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
000062  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
                  |L22.102|
;;;1269               }
;;;1270           }
;;;1271           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000066  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1272   
;;;1273           /* Force a reschedule if it is the currently running task that has just
;;;1274            * been deleted. */
;;;1275           if( xSchedulerRunning != pdFALSE )
00006a  6968              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x14]  ; xSchedulerRunning
00006c  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00006e  d00a              BEQ      |L22.134|
;;;1276           {
;;;1277               if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
000070  6828              LDR      r0,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000072  4284              CMP      r4,r0
000074  d107              BNE      |L22.134|
;;;1278               {
;;;1279                   configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1280                   portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
000076  4907              LDR      r1,|L22.148|
000078  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
00007c  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
00007e  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000082  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L22.134|
;;;1281               }
;;;1282               else
;;;1283               {
;;;1284                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1285               }
;;;1286           }
;;;1287       }
000086  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1288   
                          ENDP

                  |L22.136|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L22.140|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L22.144|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x168
                  |L22.148|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskEndScheduler||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskEndScheduler PROC
;;;2159   
;;;2160   void vTaskEndScheduler( void )
000000  20bf              MOVS     r0,#0xbf
000002  f3808811          MSR      BASEPRI,r0
000006  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00000a  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
;;;2161   {
;;;2162       /* Stop the scheduler interrupts and call the portable scheduler end
;;;2163        * routine so the original ISRs can be restored if necessary.  The port
;;;2164        * layer must ensure interrupts enable  bit is left in the correct state. */
;;;2165       portDISABLE_INTERRUPTS();
;;;2166       xSchedulerRunning = pdFALSE;
00000e  4902              LDR      r1,|L23.24|
000010  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000012  6148              STR      r0,[r1,#0x14]  ; xSchedulerRunning
;;;2167       vPortEndScheduler();
000014  f7ffbffe          B.W      vPortEndScheduler
;;;2168   }
;;;2169   /*----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L23.24|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskGenericNotifyGiveFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskGenericNotifyGiveFromISR PROC
;;;5148   
;;;5149       void vTaskGenericNotifyGiveFromISR( TaskHandle_t xTaskToNotify,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;5150                                           UBaseType_t uxIndexToNotify,
;;;5151                                           BaseType_t * pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken )
;;;5152       {
000004  4604              MOV      r4,r0
000006  4690              MOV      r8,r2
000008  20bf              MOVS     r0,#0xbf
00000a  f3ef8711          MRS      r7,BASEPRI
00000e  f3808811          MSR      BASEPRI,r0
000012  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000016  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
;;;5153           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;5154           uint8_t ucOriginalNotifyState;
;;;5155           UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;5156   
;;;5157           configASSERT( xTaskToNotify );
;;;5158           configASSERT( uxIndexToNotify < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;5159   
;;;5160           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;5161            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;5162            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;5163            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;5164            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;5165            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;5166            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;5167            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;5168            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;5169            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;5170            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;5171            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;5172            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;5173            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;5174            * provided on the following link:
;;;5175            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;5176           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;5177   
;;;5178           pxTCB = xTaskToNotify;
;;;5179   
;;;5180           uxSavedInterruptStatus = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
;;;5181           {
;;;5182               ucOriginalNotifyState = pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ];
00001a  1860              ADDS     r0,r4,r1
00001c  f8102f5c          LDRB     r2,[r0,#0x5c]!
;;;5183               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ] = taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED;
000020  2302              MOVS     r3,#2
000022  7003              STRB     r3,[r0,#0]
;;;5184   
;;;5185               /* 'Giving' is equivalent to incrementing a count in a counting
;;;5186                * semaphore. */
;;;5187               ( pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] )++;
000024  eb040081          ADD      r0,r4,r1,LSL #2
000028  6d81              LDR      r1,[r0,#0x58]
00002a  1c49              ADDS     r1,r1,#1
00002c  6581              STR      r1,[r0,#0x58]
;;;5188   
;;;5189               traceTASK_NOTIFY_GIVE_FROM_ISR( uxIndexToNotify );
;;;5190   
;;;5191               /* If the task is in the blocked state specifically to wait for a
;;;5192                * notification then unblock it now. */
;;;5193               if( ucOriginalNotifyState == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
00002e  2a01              CMP      r2,#1
000030  d113              BNE      |L24.90|
;;;5194               {
;;;5195                   /* The task should not have been on an event list. */
;;;5196                   configASSERT( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == NULL );
;;;5197   
;;;5198                   if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000032  4d14              LDR      r5,|L24.132|
000034  6b68              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x34]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000036  2601              MOVS     r6,#1                 ;5154
000038  b198              CBZ      r0,|L24.98|
;;;5199                   {
;;;5200                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
;;;5201                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
;;;5202                   }
;;;5203                   else
;;;5204                   {
;;;5205                       /* The delayed and ready lists cannot be accessed, so hold
;;;5206                        * this task pending until the scheduler is resumed. */
;;;5207                       vListInsertEnd( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
00003a  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
00003e  4812              LDR      r0,|L24.136|
                  |L24.64|
000040  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;5208                   }
;;;5209   
;;;5210                   if( pxTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000044  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000046  6829              LDR      r1,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000048  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00004a  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00004c  d905              BLS      |L24.90|
;;;5211                   {
;;;5212                       /* The notified task has a priority above the currently
;;;5213                        * executing task so a yield is required. */
;;;5214                       if( pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken != NULL )
00004e  f1b80f00          CMP      r8,#0
000052  d001              BEQ      |L24.88|
;;;5215                       {
;;;5216                           *pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken = pdTRUE;
000054  f8c86000          STR      r6,[r8,#0]
                  |L24.88|
;;;5217                       }
;;;5218   
;;;5219                       /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not
;;;5220                        * using the "xHigherPriorityTaskWoken" parameter in an ISR
;;;5221                        * safe FreeRTOS function. */
;;;5222                       xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000058  61ee              STR      r6,[r5,#0x1c]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L24.90|
00005a  f3878811          MSR      BASEPRI,r7
;;;5223                   }
;;;5224                   else
;;;5225                   {
;;;5226                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;5227                   }
;;;5228               }
;;;5229           }
;;;5230           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
;;;5231       }
00005e  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
                  |L24.98|
000062  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4              ;5200
000064  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
000068  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]         ;5201
00006a  692a              LDR      r2,[r5,#0x10]         ;5201  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00006c  fa06f100          LSL      r1,r6,r0              ;5201
000070  4311              ORRS     r1,r1,r2              ;5201
000072  6129              STR      r1,[r5,#0x10]         ;5201  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000074  eb000180          ADD      r1,r0,r0,LSL #2       ;5201
000078  4804              LDR      r0,|L24.140|
00007a  eb000081          ADD      r0,r0,r1,LSL #2       ;5201
00007e  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4              ;5201
000080  e7de              B        |L24.64|
;;;5232   
                          ENDP

000082  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L24.132|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L24.136|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x154
                  |L24.140|
                          DCD      ||.bss||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskGetInfo||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskGetInfo PROC
;;;3754   
;;;3755       void vTaskGetInfo( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;3756                          TaskStatus_t * pxTaskStatus,
;;;3757                          BaseType_t xGetFreeStackSpace,
;;;3758                          eTaskState eState )
;;;3759       {
000004  460c              MOV      r4,r1
;;;3760           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;3761   
;;;3762           /* xTask is NULL then get the state of the calling task. */
;;;3763           pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000006  491d              LDR      r1,|L25.124|
000008  4617              MOV      r7,r2                 ;3759
00000a  b1b0              CBZ      r0,|L25.58|
00000c  4605              MOV      r5,r0
                  |L25.14|
;;;3764   
;;;3765           pxTaskStatus->xHandle = ( TaskHandle_t ) pxTCB;
;;;3766           pxTaskStatus->pcTaskName = ( const char * ) &( pxTCB->pcTaskName[ 0 ] );
00000e  f1050034          ADD      r0,r5,#0x34
000012  e9c45000          STRD     r5,r0,[r4,#0]
;;;3767           pxTaskStatus->uxCurrentPriority = pxTCB->uxPriority;
000016  6ae8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x2c]
;;;3768           pxTaskStatus->pxStackBase = pxTCB->pxStack;
000018  6120              STR      r0,[r4,#0x10]
00001a  6b28              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x30]
;;;3769           pxTaskStatus->xTaskNumber = pxTCB->uxTCBNumber;
00001c  61e0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x1c]
00001e  6c68              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x44]
;;;3770   
;;;3771           #if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 )
;;;3772               {
;;;3773                   pxTaskStatus->uxBasePriority = pxTCB->uxBasePriority;
000020  60a0              STR      r0,[r4,#8]
000022  6ce8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x4c]
;;;3774               }
;;;3775           #else
;;;3776               {
;;;3777                   pxTaskStatus->uxBasePriority = 0;
;;;3778               }
;;;3779           #endif
;;;3780   
;;;3781           #if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 )
;;;3782               {
;;;3783                   pxTaskStatus->ulRunTimeCounter = pxTCB->ulRunTimeCounter;
000024  6160              STR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
000026  6d68              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x54]
000028  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
;;;3784               }
;;;3785           #else
;;;3786               {
;;;3787                   pxTaskStatus->ulRunTimeCounter = 0;
;;;3788               }
;;;3789           #endif
;;;3790   
;;;3791           /* Obtaining the task state is a little fiddly, so is only done if the
;;;3792            * value of eState passed into this function is eInvalid - otherwise the
;;;3793            * state is just set to whatever is passed in. */
;;;3794           if( eState != eInvalid )
00002a  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
00002c  2b05              CMP      r3,#5
00002e  d012              BEQ      |L25.86|
;;;3795           {
;;;3796               if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
000030  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000032  4285              CMP      r5,r0
000034  d103              BNE      |L25.62|
;;;3797               {
;;;3798                   pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eRunning;
000036  7326              STRB     r6,[r4,#0xc]
000038  e011              B        |L25.94|
                  |L25.58|
00003a  680d              LDR      r5,[r1,#0]            ;3763  ; pxCurrentTCB
00003c  e7e7              B        |L25.14|
                  |L25.62|
;;;3799               }
;;;3800               else
;;;3801               {
;;;3802                   pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eState;
00003e  7323              STRB     r3,[r4,#0xc]
;;;3803   
;;;3804                   #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;3805                       {
;;;3806                           /* If the task is in the suspended list then there is a
;;;3807                            *  chance it is actually just blocked indefinitely - so really
;;;3808                            *  it should be reported as being in the Blocked state. */
;;;3809                           if( eState == eSuspended )
000040  2b03              CMP      r3,#3
000042  d10c              BNE      |L25.94|
;;;3810                           {
;;;3811                               vTaskSuspendAll();
000044  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;3812                               {
;;;3813                                   if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
000048  6aa8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x28]
00004a  b108              CBZ      r0,|L25.80|
;;;3814                                   {
;;;3815                                       pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eBlocked;
00004c  2002              MOVS     r0,#2
00004e  7320              STRB     r0,[r4,#0xc]
                  |L25.80|
;;;3816                                   }
;;;3817                               }
;;;3818                               ( void ) xTaskResumeAll();
000050  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
000054  e003              B        |L25.94|
                  |L25.86|
;;;3819                           }
;;;3820                       }
;;;3821                   #endif /* INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend */
;;;3822               }
;;;3823           }
;;;3824           else
;;;3825           {
;;;3826               pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eTaskGetState( pxTCB );
000056  4628              MOV      r0,r5
000058  f7fffffe          BL       eTaskGetState
00005c  7320              STRB     r0,[r4,#0xc]
                  |L25.94|
;;;3827           }
;;;3828   
;;;3829           /* Obtaining the stack space takes some time, so the xGetFreeStackSpace
;;;3830            * parameter is provided to allow it to be skipped. */
;;;3831           if( xGetFreeStackSpace != pdFALSE )
00005e  b15f              CBZ      r7,|L25.120|
000060  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
;;;3832           {
;;;3833               #if ( portSTACK_GROWTH > 0 )
;;;3834                   {
;;;3835                       pxTaskStatus->usStackHighWaterMark = prvTaskCheckFreeStackSpace( ( uint8_t * ) pxTCB->pxEndOfStack );
;;;3836                   }
;;;3837               #else
;;;3838                   {
;;;3839                       pxTaskStatus->usStackHighWaterMark = prvTaskCheckFreeStackSpace( ( uint8_t * ) pxTCB->pxStack );
000062  6b28              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x30]
000064  e001              B        |L25.106|
                  |L25.102|
000066  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000068  1c49              ADDS     r1,r1,#1
                  |L25.106|
00006a  7802              LDRB     r2,[r0,#0]
00006c  2aa5              CMP      r2,#0xa5
00006e  d0fa              BEQ      |L25.102|
000070  0888              LSRS     r0,r1,#2
000072  8420              STRH     r0,[r4,#0x20]
                  |L25.116|
;;;3840                   }
;;;3841               #endif
;;;3842           }
;;;3843           else
;;;3844           {
;;;3845               pxTaskStatus->usStackHighWaterMark = 0;
;;;3846           }
;;;3847       }
000074  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
                  |L25.120|
000078  8426              STRH     r6,[r4,#0x20]         ;3845
00007a  e7fb              B        |L25.116|
;;;3848   
                          ENDP

                  |L25.124|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskGetRunTimeStats||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                          REQUIRE _printf_percent
                          REQUIRE _printf_u
                          REQUIRE _printf_int_dec
                  vTaskGetRunTimeStats PROC
;;;4570   
;;;4571       void vTaskGetRunTimeStats( char * pcWriteBuffer )
000000  e92d43f8          PUSH     {r3-r9,lr}
;;;4572       {
000004  4607              MOV      r7,r0
;;;4573           TaskStatus_t * pxTaskStatusArray;
;;;4574           UBaseType_t uxArraySize, x;
;;;4575           uint32_t ulTotalTime, ulStatsAsPercentage;
;;;4576   
;;;4577           #if ( configUSE_TRACE_FACILITY != 1 )
;;;4578               {
;;;4579                   #error configUSE_TRACE_FACILITY must also be set to 1 in FreeRTOSConfig.h to use vTaskGetRunTimeStats().
;;;4580               }
;;;4581           #endif
;;;4582   
;;;4583           /*
;;;4584            * PLEASE NOTE:
;;;4585            *
;;;4586            * This function is provided for convenience only, and is used by many
;;;4587            * of the demo applications.  Do not consider it to be part of the
;;;4588            * scheduler.
;;;4589            *
;;;4590            * vTaskGetRunTimeStats() calls uxTaskGetSystemState(), then formats part
;;;4591            * of the uxTaskGetSystemState() output into a human readable table that
;;;4592            * displays the amount of time each task has spent in the Running state
;;;4593            * in both absolute and percentage terms.
;;;4594            *
;;;4595            * vTaskGetRunTimeStats() has a dependency on the sprintf() C library
;;;4596            * function that might bloat the code size, use a lot of stack, and
;;;4597            * provide different results on different platforms.  An alternative,
;;;4598            * tiny, third party, and limited functionality implementation of
;;;4599            * sprintf() is provided in many of the FreeRTOS/Demo sub-directories in
;;;4600            * a file called printf-stdarg.c (note printf-stdarg.c does not provide
;;;4601            * a full snprintf() implementation!).
;;;4602            *
;;;4603            * It is recommended that production systems call uxTaskGetSystemState()
;;;4604            * directly to get access to raw stats data, rather than indirectly
;;;4605            * through a call to vTaskGetRunTimeStats().
;;;4606            */
;;;4607   
;;;4608           /* Make sure the write buffer does not contain a string. */
;;;4609           *pcWriteBuffer = ( char ) 0x00;
000006  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000008  7038              STRB     r0,[r7,#0]
;;;4610   
;;;4611           /* Take a snapshot of the number of tasks in case it changes while this
;;;4612            * function is executing. */
;;;4613           uxArraySize = uxCurrentNumberOfTasks;
00000a  481f              LDR      r0,|L26.136|
00000c  6884              LDR      r4,[r0,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
;;;4614   
;;;4615           /* Allocate an array index for each task.  NOTE!  If
;;;4616            * configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION is set to 0 then pvPortMalloc() will
;;;4617            * equate to NULL. */
;;;4618           pxTaskStatusArray = pvPortMalloc( uxCurrentNumberOfTasks * sizeof( TaskStatus_t ) ); /*lint !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack and this allocation allocates a struct that has the alignment requirements of a pointer. */
00000e  6880              LDR      r0,[r0,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000010  eb0000c0          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #3
000014  0080              LSLS     r0,r0,#2
000016  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
00001a  0006              MOVS     r6,r0
;;;4619   
;;;4620           if( pxTaskStatusArray != NULL )
00001c  d031              BEQ      |L26.130|
;;;4621           {
;;;4622               /* Generate the (binary) data. */
;;;4623               uxArraySize = uxTaskGetSystemState( pxTaskStatusArray, uxArraySize, &ulTotalTime );
00001e  466a              MOV      r2,sp
000020  4621              MOV      r1,r4
000022  f7fffffe          BL       uxTaskGetSystemState
000026  4681              MOV      r9,r0
;;;4624   
;;;4625               /* For percentage calculations. */
;;;4626               ulTotalTime /= 100UL;
000028  9800              LDR      r0,[sp,#0]
00002a  2164              MOVS     r1,#0x64
00002c  fbb0f0f1          UDIV     r0,r0,r1
;;;4627   
;;;4628               /* Avoid divide by zero errors. */
;;;4629               if( ulTotalTime > 0UL )
000030  9000              STR      r0,[sp,#0]
000032  b318              CBZ      r0,|L26.124|
;;;4630               {
;;;4631                   /* Create a human readable table from the binary data. */
;;;4632                   for( x = 0; x < uxArraySize; x++ )
000034  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
000036  e01f              B        |L26.120|
                  |L26.56|
;;;4633                   {
;;;4634                       /* What percentage of the total run time has the task used?
;;;4635                        * This will always be rounded down to the nearest integer.
;;;4636                        * ulTotalRunTimeDiv100 has already been divided by 100. */
;;;4637                       ulStatsAsPercentage = pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter / ulTotalTime;
000038  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
00003c  eb060580          ADD      r5,r6,r0,LSL #2
000040  9900              LDR      r1,[sp,#0]
000042  69a8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x18]
000044  fbb0f8f1          UDIV     r8,r0,r1
;;;4638   
;;;4639                       /* Write the task name to the string, padding with
;;;4640                        * spaces so it can be printed in tabular form more
;;;4641                        * easily. */
;;;4642                       pcWriteBuffer = prvWriteNameToBuffer( pcWriteBuffer, pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].pcTaskName );
000048  4638              MOV      r0,r7
00004a  6869              LDR      r1,[r5,#4]
00004c  f7fffffe          BL       prvWriteNameToBuffer
000050  4607              MOV      r7,r0
;;;4643   
;;;4644                       if( ulStatsAsPercentage > 0UL )
000052  69aa              LDR      r2,[r5,#0x18]
000054  f1b80000          SUBS     r0,r8,#0
000058  d005              BEQ      |L26.102|
;;;4645                       {
;;;4646                           #ifdef portLU_PRINTF_SPECIFIER_REQUIRED
;;;4647                               {
;;;4648                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%lu\t\t%lu%%\r\n", pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter, ulStatsAsPercentage );
;;;4649                               }
;;;4650                           #else
;;;4651                               {
;;;4652                                   /* sizeof( int ) == sizeof( long ) so a smaller
;;;4653                                    * printf() library can be used. */
;;;4654                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%u\t\t%u%%\r\n", ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter, ( unsigned int ) ulStatsAsPercentage ); /*lint !e586 sprintf() allowed as this is compiled with many compilers and this is a utility function only - not part of the core kernel implementation. */
00005a  4603              MOV      r3,r0
00005c  a10b              ADR      r1,|L26.140|
00005e  4638              MOV      r0,r7
000060  f7fffffe          BL       __2sprintf
000064  e003              B        |L26.110|
                  |L26.102|
;;;4655                               }
;;;4656                           #endif
;;;4657                       }
;;;4658                       else
;;;4659                       {
;;;4660                           /* If the percentage is zero here then the task has
;;;4661                            * consumed less than 1% of the total run time. */
;;;4662                           #ifdef portLU_PRINTF_SPECIFIER_REQUIRED
;;;4663                               {
;;;4664                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%lu\t\t<1%%\r\n", pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter );
;;;4665                               }
;;;4666                           #else
;;;4667                               {
;;;4668                                   /* sizeof( int ) == sizeof( long ) so a smaller
;;;4669                                    * printf() library can be used. */
;;;4670                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%u\t\t<1%%\r\n", ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter ); /*lint !e586 sprintf() allowed as this is compiled with many compilers and this is a utility function only - not part of the core kernel implementation. */
000066  a10c              ADR      r1,|L26.152|
000068  4638              MOV      r0,r7
00006a  f7fffffe          BL       __2sprintf
                  |L26.110|
;;;4671                               }
;;;4672                           #endif
;;;4673                       }
;;;4674   
;;;4675                       pcWriteBuffer += strlen( pcWriteBuffer ); /*lint !e9016 Pointer arithmetic ok on char pointers especially as in this case where it best denotes the intent of the code. */
00006e  4638              MOV      r0,r7
000070  f7fffffe          BL       strlen
000074  4407              ADD      r7,r7,r0
000076  1c64              ADDS     r4,r4,#1
                  |L26.120|
000078  454c              CMP      r4,r9                 ;4632
00007a  d3dd              BCC      |L26.56|
                  |L26.124|
;;;4676                   }
;;;4677               }
;;;4678               else
;;;4679               {
;;;4680                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4681               }
;;;4682   
;;;4683               /* Free the array again.  NOTE!  If configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION
;;;4684                * is 0 then vPortFree() will be #defined to nothing. */
;;;4685               vPortFree( pxTaskStatusArray );
00007c  4630              MOV      r0,r6
00007e  f7fffffe          BL       vPortFree
                  |L26.130|
;;;4686           }
;;;4687           else
;;;4688           {
;;;4689               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4690           }
;;;4691       }
000082  e8bd83f8          POP      {r3-r9,pc}
;;;4692   
                          ENDP

000086  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L26.136|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L26.140|
00008c  09257509          DCB      "\t%u\t\t%u%%\r\n",0
000090  09257525
000094  250d0a00
                  |L26.152|
000098  09257509          DCB      "\t%u\t\t<1%%\r\n",0
00009c  093c3125
0000a0  250d0a00

                          AREA ||i.vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState PROC
;;;3338   
;;;3339   void vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState( TimeOut_t * const pxTimeOut )
000000  4902              LDR      r1,|L27.12|
;;;3340   {
;;;3341       /* For internal use only as it does not use a critical section. */
;;;3342       pxTimeOut->xOverflowCount = xNumOfOverflows;
000002  6a0a              LDR      r2,[r1,#0x20]  ; xNumOfOverflows
;;;3343       pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering = xTickCount;
000004  6002              STR      r2,[r0,#0]
000006  68c9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0xc]  ; xTickCount
000008  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]
;;;3344   }
00000a  4770              BX       lr
;;;3345   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L27.12|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskIsRunning||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskIsRunning PROC
;;;5441   
;;;5442   uint8_t vTaskIsRunning(void)
000000  4802              LDR      r0,|L28.12|
;;;5443   {
;;;5444       return (xSchedulerRunning == pdFALSE)?0:1;
000002  6940              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x14]  ; xSchedulerRunning
000004  2800              CMP      r0,#0                 ;5443
000006  d000              BEQ      |L28.10|
000008  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
                  |L28.10|
;;;5445   }
00000a  4770              BX       lr
;;;5446   
                          ENDP

                  |L28.12|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                          REQUIRE _printf_percent
                          REQUIRE _printf_u
                          REQUIRE _printf_c
                          REQUIRE _printf_int_dec
                          REQUIRE _printf_str
                  vTaskList PROC
;;;4466   
;;;4467       void vTaskList( char * pcWriteBuffer )
000000  e92d47fc          PUSH     {r2-r10,lr}
;;;4468       {
;;;4469           TaskStatus_t * pxTaskStatusArray;
;;;4470           UBaseType_t uxArraySize, x;
;;;4471           char cStatus;
;;;4472   
;;;4473           /*
;;;4474            * PLEASE NOTE:
;;;4475            *
;;;4476            * This function is provided for convenience only, and is used by many
;;;4477            * of the demo applications.  Do not consider it to be part of the
;;;4478            * scheduler.
;;;4479            *
;;;4480            * vTaskList() calls uxTaskGetSystemState(), then formats part of the
;;;4481            * uxTaskGetSystemState() output into a human readable table that
;;;4482            * displays task names, states and stack usage.
;;;4483            *
;;;4484            * vTaskList() has a dependency on the sprintf() C library function that
;;;4485            * might bloat the code size, use a lot of stack, and provide different
;;;4486            * results on different platforms.  An alternative, tiny, third party,
;;;4487            * and limited functionality implementation of sprintf() is provided in
;;;4488            * many of the FreeRTOS/Demo sub-directories in a file called
;;;4489            * printf-stdarg.c (note printf-stdarg.c does not provide a full
;;;4490            * snprintf() implementation!).
;;;4491            *
;;;4492            * It is recommended that production systems call uxTaskGetSystemState()
;;;4493            * directly to get access to raw stats data, rather than indirectly
;;;4494            * through a call to vTaskList().
;;;4495            */
;;;4496   
;;;4497   
;;;4498           /* Make sure the write buffer does not contain a string. */
;;;4499           *pcWriteBuffer = ( char ) 0x00;
000004  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
000006  4680              MOV      r8,r0                 ;4468
000008  7001              STRB     r1,[r0,#0]
;;;4500   
;;;4501           /* Take a snapshot of the number of tasks in case it changes while this
;;;4502            * function is executing. */
;;;4503           uxArraySize = uxCurrentNumberOfTasks;
00000a  4822              LDR      r0,|L29.148|
00000c  6884              LDR      r4,[r0,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
;;;4504   
;;;4505           /* Allocate an array index for each task.  NOTE!  if
;;;4506            * configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION is set to 0 then pvPortMalloc() will
;;;4507            * equate to NULL. */
;;;4508           pxTaskStatusArray = pvPortMalloc( uxCurrentNumberOfTasks * sizeof( TaskStatus_t ) ); /*lint !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack and this allocation allocates a struct that has the alignment requirements of a pointer. */
00000e  6880              LDR      r0,[r0,#8]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000010  eb0000c0          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #3
000014  0080              LSLS     r0,r0,#2
000016  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
00001a  0007              MOVS     r7,r0
;;;4509   
;;;4510           if( pxTaskStatusArray != NULL )
00001c  d038              BEQ      |L29.144|
;;;4511           {
;;;4512               /* Generate the (binary) data. */
;;;4513               uxArraySize = uxTaskGetSystemState( pxTaskStatusArray, uxArraySize, NULL );
00001e  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
000020  4621              MOV      r1,r4
000022  f7fffffe          BL       uxTaskGetSystemState
000026  4681              MOV      r9,r0
;;;4514   
;;;4515               /* Create a human readable table from the binary data. */
;;;4516               for( x = 0; x < uxArraySize; x++ )
000028  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
00002a  e02a              B        |L29.130|
                  |L29.44|
;;;4517               {
;;;4518                   switch( pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].eCurrentState )
00002c  eb0600c6          ADD      r0,r6,r6,LSL #3
000030  eb070580          ADD      r5,r7,r0,LSL #2
000034  7b28              LDRB     r0,[r5,#0xc]
000036  2805              CMP      r0,#5
000038  d20e              BCS      |L29.88|
00003a  e8dff000          TBB      [pc,r0]
00003e  0305              DCB      0x03,0x05
000040  07090b00          DCB      0x07,0x09,0x0b,0x00
;;;4519                   {
;;;4520                       case eRunning:
;;;4521                           cStatus = tskRUNNING_CHAR;
000044  2458              MOVS     r4,#0x58
;;;4522                           break;
000046  e008              B        |L29.90|
;;;4523   
;;;4524                       case eReady:
;;;4525                           cStatus = tskREADY_CHAR;
000048  2452              MOVS     r4,#0x52
;;;4526                           break;
00004a  e006              B        |L29.90|
;;;4527   
;;;4528                       case eBlocked:
;;;4529                           cStatus = tskBLOCKED_CHAR;
00004c  2442              MOVS     r4,#0x42
;;;4530                           break;
00004e  e004              B        |L29.90|
;;;4531   
;;;4532                       case eSuspended:
;;;4533                           cStatus = tskSUSPENDED_CHAR;
000050  2453              MOVS     r4,#0x53
;;;4534                           break;
000052  e002              B        |L29.90|
;;;4535   
;;;4536                       case eDeleted:
;;;4537                           cStatus = tskDELETED_CHAR;
000054  2444              MOVS     r4,#0x44
;;;4538                           break;
000056  e000              B        |L29.90|
                  |L29.88|
;;;4539   
;;;4540                       case eInvalid: /* Fall through. */
;;;4541                       default:       /* Should not get here, but it is included
;;;4542                                       * to prevent static checking errors. */
;;;4543                           cStatus = ( char ) 0x00;
000058  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
                  |L29.90|
;;;4544                           break;
;;;4545                   }
;;;4546   
;;;4547                   /* Write the task name to the string, padding with spaces so it
;;;4548                    * can be printed in tabular form more easily. */
;;;4549                   pcWriteBuffer = prvWriteNameToBuffer( pcWriteBuffer, pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].pcTaskName );
00005a  4640              MOV      r0,r8
00005c  6869              LDR      r1,[r5,#4]
00005e  f7fffffe          BL       prvWriteNameToBuffer
000062  4680              MOV      r8,r0
;;;4550   
;;;4551                   /* Write the rest of the string. */
;;;4552                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%c\t%u\t%u\t%u\r\n", cStatus, ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].uxCurrentPriority, ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].usStackHighWaterMark, ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].xTaskNumber ); /*lint !e586 sprintf() allowed as this is compiled with many compilers and this is a utility function only - not part of the core kernel implementation. */
000064  68a9              LDR      r1,[r5,#8]
000066  8c28              LDRH     r0,[r5,#0x20]
000068  e9cd0100          STRD     r0,r1,[sp,#0]
00006c  4622              MOV      r2,r4
00006e  a10a              ADR      r1,|L29.152|
000070  4640              MOV      r0,r8
000072  692b              LDR      r3,[r5,#0x10]
000074  f7fffffe          BL       __2sprintf
;;;4553                   pcWriteBuffer += strlen( pcWriteBuffer );                                                                                                                                                                                                /*lint !e9016 Pointer arithmetic ok on char pointers especially as in this case where it best denotes the intent of the code. */
000078  4640              MOV      r0,r8
00007a  f7fffffe          BL       strlen
00007e  4480              ADD      r8,r8,r0
000080  1c76              ADDS     r6,r6,#1
                  |L29.130|
000082  454e              CMP      r6,r9                 ;4516
000084  d3d2              BCC      |L29.44|
;;;4554               }
;;;4555   
;;;4556               /* Free the array again.  NOTE!  If configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION
;;;4557                * is 0 then vPortFree() will be #defined to nothing. */
;;;4558               vPortFree( pxTaskStatusArray );
000086  4638              MOV      r0,r7
000088  e8bd47fc          POP      {r2-r10,lr}
00008c  f7ffbffe          B.W      vPortFree
                  |L29.144|
;;;4559           }
;;;4560           else
;;;4561           {
;;;4562               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4563           }
;;;4564       }
000090  e8bd87fc          POP      {r2-r10,pc}
;;;4565   
                          ENDP

                  |L29.148|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L29.152|
000098  09256309          DCB      "\t%c\t%u\t%u\t%u\r\n",0
00009c  25750925
0000a0  75092575
0000a4  0d0a00  
0000a7  00                DCB      0

                          AREA ||i.vTaskMissedYield||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskMissedYield PROC
;;;3411   
;;;3412   void vTaskMissedYield( void )
000000  4901              LDR      r1,|L30.8|
;;;3413   {
;;;3414       xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000002  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000004  61c8              STR      r0,[r1,#0x1c]  ; xYieldPending
;;;3415   }
000006  4770              BX       lr
;;;3416   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L30.8|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPlaceOnEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPlaceOnEventList PROC
;;;3130   
;;;3131   void vTaskPlaceOnEventList( List_t * const pxEventList,
000000  4602              MOV      r2,r0
;;;3132                               const TickType_t xTicksToWait )
;;;3133   {
000002  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;3134       configASSERT( pxEventList );
;;;3135   
;;;3136       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED WITH EITHER INTERRUPTS DISABLED OR THE
;;;3137        * SCHEDULER SUSPENDED AND THE QUEUE BEING ACCESSED LOCKED. */
;;;3138   
;;;3139       /* Place the event list item of the TCB in the appropriate event list.
;;;3140        * This is placed in the list in priority order so the highest priority task
;;;3141        * is the first to be woken by the event.  The queue that contains the event
;;;3142        * list is locked, preventing simultaneous access from interrupts. */
;;;3143       vListInsert( pxEventList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000004  4806              LDR      r0,|L31.32|
000006  460c              MOV      r4,r1                 ;3133
000008  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000a  4610              MOV      r0,r2
00000c  3118              ADDS     r1,r1,#0x18
00000e  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsert
;;;3144   
;;;3145       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToWait, pdTRUE );
000012  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000014  e8bd4010          POP      {r4,lr}
000018  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
00001a  f7ffbffe          B.W      prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;3146   }
;;;3147   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

00001e  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L31.32|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPlaceOnUnorderedEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPlaceOnUnorderedEventList PROC
;;;3148   
;;;3149   void vTaskPlaceOnUnorderedEventList( List_t * pxEventList,
000000  4603              MOV      r3,r0
;;;3150                                        const TickType_t xItemValue,
;;;3151                                        const TickType_t xTicksToWait )
;;;3152   {
;;;3153       configASSERT( pxEventList );
;;;3154   
;;;3155       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED WITH THE SCHEDULER SUSPENDED.  It is used by
;;;3156        * the event groups implementation. */
;;;3157       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended != 0 );
;;;3158   
;;;3159       /* Store the item value in the event list item.  It is safe to access the
;;;3160        * event list item here as interrupts won't access the event list item of a
;;;3161        * task that is not in the Blocked state. */
;;;3162       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ), xItemValue | taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE );
000002  f0414000          ORR      r0,r1,#0x80000000
000006  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}               ;3152
000008  4907              LDR      r1,|L32.40|
00000a  4614              MOV      r4,r2                 ;3152
00000c  680a              LDR      r2,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
;;;3163   
;;;3164       /* Place the event list item of the TCB at the end of the appropriate event
;;;3165        * list.  It is safe to access the event list here because it is part of an
;;;3166        * event group implementation - and interrupts don't access event groups
;;;3167        * directly (instead they access them indirectly by pending function calls to
;;;3168        * the task level). */
;;;3169       vListInsertEnd( pxEventList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ) );
00000e  6190              STR      r0,[r2,#0x18]
000010  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000012  4618              MOV      r0,r3
000014  3118              ADDS     r1,r1,#0x18
000016  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;3170   
;;;3171       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToWait, pdTRUE );
00001a  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00001c  e8bd4010          POP      {r4,lr}
000020  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000022  f7ffbffe          B.W      prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;3172   }
;;;3173   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000026  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L32.40|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPriorityDisinheritAfterTimeout||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPriorityDisinheritAfterTimeout PROC
;;;4270   
;;;4271       void vTaskPriorityDisinheritAfterTimeout( TaskHandle_t const pxMutexHolder,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;4272                                                 UBaseType_t uxHighestPriorityWaitingTask )
;;;4273       {
000002  460a              MOV      r2,r1
;;;4274           TCB_t * const pxTCB = pxMutexHolder;
000004  0004              MOVS     r4,r0
000006  d033              BEQ      |L33.112|
;;;4275           UBaseType_t uxPriorityUsedOnEntry, uxPriorityToUse;
;;;4276           const UBaseType_t uxOnlyOneMutexHeld = ( UBaseType_t ) 1;
;;;4277   
;;;4278           if( pxMutexHolder != NULL )
;;;4279           {
;;;4280               /* If pxMutexHolder is not NULL then the holder must hold at least
;;;4281                * one mutex. */
;;;4282               configASSERT( pxTCB->uxMutexesHeld );
;;;4283   
;;;4284               /* Determine the priority to which the priority of the task that
;;;4285                * holds the mutex should be set.  This will be the greater of the
;;;4286                * holding task's base priority and the priority of the highest
;;;4287                * priority task that is waiting to obtain the mutex. */
;;;4288               if( pxTCB->uxBasePriority < uxHighestPriorityWaitingTask )
000008  6ce1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x4c]
00000a  4291              CMP      r1,r2
00000c  d200              BCS      |L33.16|
;;;4289               {
;;;4290                   uxPriorityToUse = uxHighestPriorityWaitingTask;
00000e  4611              MOV      r1,r2
                  |L33.16|
;;;4291               }
;;;4292               else
;;;4293               {
;;;4294                   uxPriorityToUse = pxTCB->uxBasePriority;
;;;4295               }
;;;4296   
;;;4297               /* Does the priority need to change? */
;;;4298               if( pxTCB->uxPriority != uxPriorityToUse )
000010  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000012  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000014  d02c              BEQ      |L33.112|
;;;4299               {
;;;4300                   /* Only disinherit if no other mutexes are held.  This is a
;;;4301                    * simplification in the priority inheritance implementation.  If
;;;4302                    * the task that holds the mutex is also holding other mutexes then
;;;4303                    * the other mutexes may have caused the priority inheritance. */
;;;4304                   if( pxTCB->uxMutexesHeld == uxOnlyOneMutexHeld )
000016  6d22              LDR      r2,[r4,#0x50]
000018  2a01              CMP      r2,#1
00001a  d129              BNE      |L33.112|
;;;4305                   {
;;;4306                       /* If a task has timed out because it already holds the
;;;4307                        * mutex it was trying to obtain then it cannot of inherited
;;;4308                        * its own priority. */
;;;4309                       configASSERT( pxTCB != pxCurrentTCB );
;;;4310   
;;;4311                       /* Disinherit the priority, remembering the previous
;;;4312                        * priority to facilitate determining the subject task's
;;;4313                        * state. */
;;;4314                       traceTASK_PRIORITY_DISINHERIT( pxTCB, uxPriorityToUse );
;;;4315                       uxPriorityUsedOnEntry = pxTCB->uxPriority;
;;;4316                       pxTCB->uxPriority = uxPriorityToUse;
;;;4317   
;;;4318                       /* Only reset the event list item value if the value is not
;;;4319                        * being used for anything else. */
;;;4320                       if( ( listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) & taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE ) == 0UL )
00001c  62e1              STR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00001e  69a2              LDR      r2,[r4,#0x18]
000020  2a00              CMP      r2,#0
000022  db02              BLT      |L33.42|
;;;4321                       {
;;;4322                           listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ), ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) uxPriorityToUse ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000024  f1c1010f          RSB      r1,r1,#0xf
000028  61a1              STR      r1,[r4,#0x18]
                  |L33.42|
;;;4323                       }
;;;4324                       else
;;;4325                       {
;;;4326                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4327                       }
;;;4328   
;;;4329                       /* If the running task is not the task that holds the mutex
;;;4330                        * then the task that holds the mutex could be in either the
;;;4331                        * Ready, Blocked or Suspended states.  Only remove the task
;;;4332                        * from its current state list if it is in the Ready state as
;;;4333                        * the task's priority is going to change and there is one
;;;4334                        * Ready list per priority. */
;;;4335                       if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ uxPriorityUsedOnEntry ] ), &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) != pdFALSE )
00002a  4d12              LDR      r5,|L33.116|
00002c  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
000030  6961              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x14]
000032  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
000036  4281              CMP      r1,r0
000038  d11a              BNE      |L33.112|
;;;4336                       {
;;;4337                           if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
00003a  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00003c  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;4338                           {
;;;4339                               /* It is known that the task is in its ready list so
;;;4340                                * there is no need to check again and the port level
;;;4341                                * reset macro can be called directly. */
;;;4342                               portRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxTCB->uxPriority, uxTopReadyPriority );
000040  490d              LDR      r1,|L33.120|
000042  2201              MOVS     r2,#1                 ;4276
000044  b930              CBNZ     r0,|L33.84|
000046  f894002c          LDRB     r0,[r4,#0x2c]
00004a  690b              LDR      r3,[r1,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00004c  fa02f000          LSL      r0,r2,r0
000050  4383              BICS     r3,r3,r0
000052  610b              STR      r3,[r1,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L33.84|
;;;4343                           }
;;;4344                           else
;;;4345                           {
;;;4346                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4347                           }
;;;4348   
;;;4349                           prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000054  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000056  690b              LDR      r3,[r1,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000058  4082              LSLS     r2,r2,r0
00005a  431a              ORRS     r2,r2,r3
00005c  610a              STR      r2,[r1,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00005e  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
000062  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
000066  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000068  e8bd4070          POP      {r4-r6,lr}
00006c  f7ffbffe          B.W      vListInsertEnd
                  |L33.112|
;;;4350                       }
;;;4351                       else
;;;4352                       {
;;;4353                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4354                       }
;;;4355                   }
;;;4356                   else
;;;4357                   {
;;;4358                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4359                   }
;;;4360               }
;;;4361               else
;;;4362               {
;;;4363                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4364               }
;;;4365           }
;;;4366           else
;;;4367           {
;;;4368               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4369           }
;;;4370       }
000070  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;4371   
                          ENDP

000072  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L33.116|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L33.120|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPrioritySet||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPrioritySet PROC
;;;1584   
;;;1585       void vTaskPrioritySet( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;1586                              UBaseType_t uxNewPriority )
;;;1587       {
;;;1588           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;1589           UBaseType_t uxCurrentBasePriority, uxPriorityUsedOnEntry;
;;;1590           BaseType_t xYieldRequired = pdFALSE;
000004  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
000006  460d              MOV      r5,r1                 ;1587
000008  4604              MOV      r4,r0                 ;1587
;;;1591   
;;;1592           configASSERT( ( uxNewPriority < configMAX_PRIORITIES ) );
;;;1593   
;;;1594           /* Ensure the new priority is valid. */
;;;1595           if( uxNewPriority >= ( UBaseType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES )
00000a  290f              CMP      r1,#0xf
00000c  d300              BCC      |L34.16|
;;;1596           {
;;;1597               uxNewPriority = ( UBaseType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( UBaseType_t ) 1U;
00000e  250e              MOVS     r5,#0xe
                  |L34.16|
;;;1598           }
;;;1599           else
;;;1600           {
;;;1601               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1602           }
;;;1603   
;;;1604           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000010  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1605           {
;;;1606               /* If null is passed in here then it is the priority of the calling
;;;1607                * task that is being changed. */
;;;1608               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000014  4f24              LDR      r7,|L34.168|
000016  b904              CBNZ     r4,|L34.26|
000018  683c              LDR      r4,[r7,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
                  |L34.26|
;;;1609   
;;;1610               traceTASK_PRIORITY_SET( pxTCB, uxNewPriority );
;;;1611   
;;;1612               #if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 )
;;;1613                   {
;;;1614                       uxCurrentBasePriority = pxTCB->uxBasePriority;
;;;1615                   }
;;;1616               #else
;;;1617                   {
;;;1618                       uxCurrentBasePriority = pxTCB->uxPriority;
;;;1619                   }
;;;1620               #endif
;;;1621   
;;;1622               if( uxCurrentBasePriority != uxNewPriority )
00001a  6ce0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x4c]
00001c  42a8              CMP      r0,r5
00001e  d03f              BEQ      |L34.160|
;;;1623               {
;;;1624                   /* The priority change may have readied a task of higher
;;;1625                    * priority than the calling task. */
;;;1626                   if( uxNewPriority > uxCurrentBasePriority )
;;;1627                   {
;;;1628                       if( pxTCB != pxCurrentTCB )
;;;1629                       {
;;;1630                           /* The priority of a task other than the currently
;;;1631                            * running task is being raised.  Is the priority being
;;;1632                            * raised above that of the running task? */
;;;1633                           if( uxNewPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
;;;1634                           {
;;;1635                               xYieldRequired = pdTRUE;
;;;1636                           }
;;;1637                           else
;;;1638                           {
;;;1639                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1640                           }
;;;1641                       }
;;;1642                       else
;;;1643                       {
;;;1644                           /* The priority of the running task is being raised,
;;;1645                            * but the running task must already be the highest
;;;1646                            * priority task able to run so no yield is required. */
;;;1647                       }
;;;1648                   }
;;;1649                   else if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
000020  6839              LDR      r1,[r7,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000022  4285              CMP      r5,r0                 ;1626
000024  d906              BLS      |L34.52|
000026  428c              CMP      r4,r1                 ;1628
000028  d007              BEQ      |L34.58|
00002a  6839              LDR      r1,[r7,#0]            ;1633  ; pxCurrentTCB
00002c  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]         ;1633
00002e  42a9              CMP      r1,r5                 ;1633
000030  d902              BLS      |L34.56|
000032  e002              B        |L34.58|
                  |L34.52|
000034  428c              CMP      r4,r1
000036  d100              BNE      |L34.58|
                  |L34.56|
;;;1650                   {
;;;1651                       /* Setting the priority of the running task down means
;;;1652                        * there may now be another task of higher priority that
;;;1653                        * is ready to execute. */
;;;1654                       xYieldRequired = pdTRUE;
000038  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
                  |L34.58|
;;;1655                   }
;;;1656                   else
;;;1657                   {
;;;1658                       /* Setting the priority of any other task down does not
;;;1659                        * require a yield as the running task must be above the
;;;1660                        * new priority of the task being modified. */
;;;1661                   }
;;;1662   
;;;1663                   /* Remember the ready list the task might be referenced from
;;;1664                    * before its uxPriority member is changed so the
;;;1665                    * taskRESET_READY_PRIORITY() macro can function correctly. */
;;;1666                   uxPriorityUsedOnEntry = pxTCB->uxPriority;
00003a  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00003c  4688              MOV      r8,r1
;;;1667   
;;;1668                   #if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 )
;;;1669                       {
;;;1670                           /* Only change the priority being used if the task is not
;;;1671                            * currently using an inherited priority. */
;;;1672                           if( pxTCB->uxBasePriority == pxTCB->uxPriority )
00003e  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000040  d100              BNE      |L34.68|
;;;1673                           {
;;;1674                               pxTCB->uxPriority = uxNewPriority;
000042  62e5              STR      r5,[r4,#0x2c]
                  |L34.68|
;;;1675                           }
;;;1676                           else
;;;1677                           {
;;;1678                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1679                           }
;;;1680   
;;;1681                           /* The base priority gets set whatever. */
;;;1682                           pxTCB->uxBasePriority = uxNewPriority;
;;;1683                       }
;;;1684                   #else /* if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 ) */
;;;1685                       {
;;;1686                           pxTCB->uxPriority = uxNewPriority;
;;;1687                       }
;;;1688                   #endif /* if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 ) */
;;;1689   
;;;1690                   /* Only reset the event list item value if the value is not
;;;1691                    * being used for anything else. */
;;;1692                   if( ( listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) & taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE ) == 0UL )
000044  64e5              STR      r5,[r4,#0x4c]
000046  69a0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
000048  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00004a  db02              BLT      |L34.82|
;;;1693                   {
;;;1694                       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ), ( ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) uxNewPriority ) ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
00004c  f1c5000f          RSB      r0,r5,#0xf
000050  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
                  |L34.82|
;;;1695                   }
;;;1696                   else
;;;1697                   {
;;;1698                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1699                   }
;;;1700   
;;;1701                   /* If the task is in the blocked or suspended list we need do
;;;1702                    * nothing more than change its priority variable. However, if
;;;1703                    * the task is in a ready list it needs to be removed and placed
;;;1704                    * in the list appropriate to its new priority. */
;;;1705                   if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ uxPriorityUsedOnEntry ] ), &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) != pdFALSE )
000052  4d16              LDR      r5,|L34.172|
000054  eb080088          ADD      r0,r8,r8,LSL #2
000058  6961              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x14]
00005a  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
00005e  4281              CMP      r1,r0
000060  d115              BNE      |L34.142|
;;;1706                   {
;;;1707                       /* The task is currently in its ready list - remove before
;;;1708                        * adding it to it's new ready list.  As we are in a critical
;;;1709                        * section we can do this even if the scheduler is suspended. */
;;;1710                       if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000062  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000064  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
000068  2101              MOVS     r1,#1                 ;1635
00006a  b920              CBNZ     r0,|L34.118|
;;;1711                       {
;;;1712                           /* It is known that the task is in its ready list so
;;;1713                            * there is no need to check again and the port level
;;;1714                            * reset macro can be called directly. */
;;;1715                           portRESET_READY_PRIORITY( uxPriorityUsedOnEntry, uxTopReadyPriority );
00006c  693a              LDR      r2,[r7,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00006e  fa01f008          LSL      r0,r1,r8
000072  4382              BICS     r2,r2,r0
000074  613a              STR      r2,[r7,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L34.118|
;;;1716                       }
;;;1717                       else
;;;1718                       {
;;;1719                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1720                       }
;;;1721   
;;;1722                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000076  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000078  693a              LDR      r2,[r7,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00007a  4081              LSLS     r1,r1,r0
00007c  4311              ORRS     r1,r1,r2
00007e  6139              STR      r1,[r7,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000080  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
000084  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
000088  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00008a  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L34.142|
;;;1723                   }
;;;1724                   else
;;;1725                   {
;;;1726                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1727                   }
;;;1728   
;;;1729                   if( xYieldRequired != pdFALSE )
00008e  b13e              CBZ      r6,|L34.160|
;;;1730                   {
;;;1731                       taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
000090  4907              LDR      r1,|L34.176|
000092  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000096  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000098  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00009c  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L34.160|
;;;1732                   }
;;;1733                   else
;;;1734                   {
;;;1735                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1736                   }
;;;1737   
;;;1738                   /* Remove compiler warning about unused variables when the port
;;;1739                    * optimised task selection is not being used. */
;;;1740                   ( void ) uxPriorityUsedOnEntry;
;;;1741               }
;;;1742           }
;;;1743           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
0000a0  e8bd41f0          POP      {r4-r8,lr}
0000a4  f7ffbffe          B.W      vPortExitCritical
;;;1744       }
;;;1745   
                          ENDP

                  |L34.168|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L34.172|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L34.176|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskRemoveFromUnorderedEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskRemoveFromUnorderedEventList PROC
;;;3277   
;;;3278   void vTaskRemoveFromUnorderedEventList( ListItem_t * pxEventListItem,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;3279                                           const TickType_t xItemValue )
;;;3280   {
;;;3281       TCB_t * pxUnblockedTCB;
;;;3282   
;;;3283       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED WITH THE SCHEDULER SUSPENDED.  It is used by
;;;3284        * the event flags implementation. */
;;;3285       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended != pdFALSE );
;;;3286   
;;;3287       /* Store the new item value in the event list. */
;;;3288       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( pxEventListItem, xItemValue | taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE );
000002  f0414100          ORR      r1,r1,#0x80000000
;;;3289   
;;;3290       /* Remove the event list form the event flag.  Interrupts do not access
;;;3291        * event flags. */
;;;3292       pxUnblockedTCB = listGET_LIST_ITEM_OWNER( pxEventListItem ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000006  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;3293       configASSERT( pxUnblockedTCB );
;;;3294       ( void ) uxListRemove( pxEventListItem );
000008  68c4              LDR      r4,[r0,#0xc]
00000a  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;3295   
;;;3296       #if ( configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE != 0 )
;;;3297           {
;;;3298               /* If a task is blocked on a kernel object then xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;3299                * might be set to the blocked task's time out time.  If the task is
;;;3300                * unblocked for a reason other than a timeout xNextTaskUnblockTime is
;;;3301                * normally left unchanged, because it is automatically reset to a new
;;;3302                * value when the tick count equals xNextTaskUnblockTime.  However if
;;;3303                * tickless idling is used it might be more important to enter sleep mode
;;;3304                * at the earliest possible time - so reset xNextTaskUnblockTime here to
;;;3305                * ensure it is updated at the earliest possible time. */
;;;3306               prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
;;;3307           }
;;;3308       #endif
;;;3309   
;;;3310       /* Remove the task from the delayed list and add it to the ready list.  The
;;;3311        * scheduler is suspended so interrupts will not be accessing the ready
;;;3312        * lists. */
;;;3313       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxUnblockedTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00000e  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000010  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;3314       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxUnblockedTCB );
000014  4d0b              LDR      r5,|L35.68|
000016  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000018  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
00001a  692a              LDR      r2,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00001c  fa06f100          LSL      r1,r6,r0
000020  4311              ORRS     r1,r1,r2
000022  6129              STR      r1,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000024  4908              LDR      r1,|L35.72|
000026  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
00002a  eb010080          ADD      r0,r1,r0,LSL #2
00002e  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000030  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;3315   
;;;3316       if( pxUnblockedTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000034  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000036  6829              LDR      r1,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000038  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00003a  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00003c  d900              BLS      |L35.64|
;;;3317       {
;;;3318           /* The unblocked task has a priority above that of the calling task, so
;;;3319            * a context switch is required.  This function is called with the
;;;3320            * scheduler suspended so xYieldPending is set so the context switch
;;;3321            * occurs immediately that the scheduler is resumed (unsuspended). */
;;;3322           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
