00003e  61ee              STR      r6,[r5,#0x1c]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L35.64|
;;;3323       }
;;;3324   }
000040  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;3325   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000042  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L35.68|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L35.72|
                          DCD      ||.bss||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskResume||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskResume PROC
;;;1902   
;;;1903       void vTaskResume( TaskHandle_t xTaskToResume )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1904       {
;;;1905           TCB_t * const pxTCB = xTaskToResume;
;;;1906   
;;;1907           /* It does not make sense to resume the calling task. */
;;;1908           configASSERT( xTaskToResume );
;;;1909   
;;;1910           /* The parameter cannot be NULL as it is impossible to resume the
;;;1911            * currently executing task. */
;;;1912           if( ( pxTCB != pxCurrentTCB ) && ( pxTCB != NULL ) )
000002  4d18              LDR      r5,|L36.100|
000004  4604              MOV      r4,r0                 ;1904
000006  6828              LDR      r0,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000008  4284              CMP      r4,r0
00000a  d029              BEQ      |L36.96|
00000c  2c00              CMP      r4,#0
00000e  d027              BEQ      |L36.96|
;;;1913           {
;;;1914               taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000010  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1915               {
;;;1916                   if( prvTaskIsTaskSuspended( pxTCB ) != pdFALSE )
000014  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000016  f7fffffe          BL       prvTaskIsTaskSuspended
00001a  b1e8              CBZ      r0,|L36.88|
;;;1917                   {
;;;1918                       traceTASK_RESUME( pxTCB );
;;;1919   
;;;1920                       /* The ready list can be accessed even if the scheduler is
;;;1921                        * suspended because this is inside a critical section. */
;;;1922                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00001c  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00001e  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;1923                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000022  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000024  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000026  692a              LDR      r2,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000028  4081              LSLS     r1,r1,r0
00002a  4311              ORRS     r1,r1,r2
00002c  6129              STR      r1,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00002e  490e              LDR      r1,|L36.104|
000030  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
000034  eb010080          ADD      r0,r1,r0,LSL #2
000038  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00003a  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;1924   
;;;1925                       /* A higher priority task may have just been resumed. */
;;;1926                       if( pxTCB->uxPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
00003e  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000040  6829              LDR      r1,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000042  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000044  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000046  d307              BCC      |L36.88|
;;;1927                       {
;;;1928                           /* This yield may not cause the task just resumed to run,
;;;1929                            * but will leave the lists in the correct state for the
;;;1930                            * next yield. */
;;;1931                           taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
000048  4908              LDR      r1,|L36.108|
00004a  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
00004e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000050  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000054  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L36.88|
;;;1932                       }
;;;1933                       else
;;;1934                       {
;;;1935                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1936                       }
;;;1937                   }
;;;1938                   else
;;;1939                   {
;;;1940                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1941                   }
;;;1942               }
;;;1943               taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000058  e8bd4070          POP      {r4-r6,lr}
00005c  f7ffbffe          B.W      vPortExitCritical
                  |L36.96|
;;;1944           }
;;;1945           else
;;;1946           {
;;;1947               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1948           }
;;;1949       }
000060  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1950   
                          ENDP

000062  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L36.100|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L36.104|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L36.108|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSetTaskNumber||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  vTaskSetTaskNumber PROC
;;;3442   
;;;3443       void vTaskSetTaskNumber( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  2800              CMP      r0,#0
;;;3444                                const UBaseType_t uxHandle )
;;;3445       {
000002  d000              BEQ      |L37.6|
;;;3446           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;3447   
;;;3448           if( xTask != NULL )
;;;3449           {
;;;3450               pxTCB = xTask;
;;;3451               pxTCB->uxTaskNumber = uxHandle;
000004  6481              STR      r1,[r0,#0x48]
                  |L37.6|
;;;3452           }
;;;3453       }
000006  4770              BX       lr
;;;3454   
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.vTaskSetTimeOutState||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSetTimeOutState PROC
;;;3326   
;;;3327   void vTaskSetTimeOutState( TimeOut_t * const pxTimeOut )
000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;3328   {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;3329       configASSERT( pxTimeOut );
;;;3330       taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;3331       {
;;;3332           pxTimeOut->xOverflowCount = xNumOfOverflows;
000008  4804              LDR      r0,|L38.28|
00000a  6a01              LDR      r1,[r0,#0x20]  ; xNumOfOverflows
;;;3333           pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering = xTickCount;
00000c  6021              STR      r1,[r4,#0]
00000e  68c0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0xc]  ; xTickCount
;;;3334       }
;;;3335       taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000010  6060              STR      r0,[r4,#4]
000012  e8bd4010          POP      {r4,lr}
000016  f7ffbffe          B.W      vPortExitCritical
;;;3336   }
;;;3337   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

00001a  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L38.28|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskStartScheduler||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskStartScheduler PROC
;;;2031   
;;;2032   void vTaskStartScheduler( void )
000000  b57c              PUSH     {r2-r6,lr}
;;;2033   {
;;;2034       BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;2035   
;;;2036       /* Add the idle task at the lowest priority. */
;;;2037       #if ( configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION == 1 )
;;;2038           {
;;;2039               StaticTask_t * pxIdleTaskTCBBuffer = NULL;
;;;2040               StackType_t * pxIdleTaskStackBuffer = NULL;
;;;2041               uint32_t ulIdleTaskStackSize;
;;;2042   
;;;2043               /* The Idle task is created using user provided RAM - obtain the
;;;2044                * address of the RAM then create the idle task. */
;;;2045               vApplicationGetIdleTaskMemory( &pxIdleTaskTCBBuffer, &pxIdleTaskStackBuffer, &ulIdleTaskStackSize );
;;;2046               xIdleTaskHandle = xTaskCreateStatic( prvIdleTask,
;;;2047                                                    configIDLE_TASK_NAME,
;;;2048                                                    ulIdleTaskStackSize,
;;;2049                                                    ( void * ) NULL,       /*lint !e961.  The cast is not redundant for all compilers. */
;;;2050                                                    portPRIVILEGE_BIT,     /* In effect ( tskIDLE_PRIORITY | portPRIVILEGE_BIT ), but tskIDLE_PRIORITY is zero. */
;;;2051                                                    pxIdleTaskStackBuffer,
;;;2052                                                    pxIdleTaskTCBBuffer ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception, justified as it is not a redundant explicit cast to all supported compilers. */
;;;2053   
;;;2054               if( xIdleTaskHandle != NULL )
;;;2055               {
;;;2056                   xReturn = pdPASS;
;;;2057               }
;;;2058               else
;;;2059               {
;;;2060                   xReturn = pdFAIL;
;;;2061               }
;;;2062           }
;;;2063       #else /* if ( configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION == 1 ) */
;;;2064           {
;;;2065               /* The Idle task is being created using dynamically allocated RAM. */
;;;2066               xReturn = xTaskCreate( prvIdleTask,
000002  4810              LDR      r0,|L39.68|
000004  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
000006  e9cd5000          STRD     r5,r0,[sp,#0]
00000a  462b              MOV      r3,r5
00000c  2280              MOVS     r2,#0x80
00000e  a10e              ADR      r1,|L39.72|
000010  480f              LDR      r0,|L39.80|
000012  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskCreate
;;;2067                                      configIDLE_TASK_NAME,
;;;2068                                      configMINIMAL_STACK_SIZE,
;;;2069                                      ( void * ) NULL,
;;;2070                                      portPRIVILEGE_BIT,  /* In effect ( tskIDLE_PRIORITY | portPRIVILEGE_BIT ), but tskIDLE_PRIORITY is zero. */
;;;2071                                      &xIdleTaskHandle ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception, justified as it is not a redundant explicit cast to all supported compilers. */
;;;2072           }
;;;2073       #endif /* configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION */
;;;2074   
;;;2075       #if ( configUSE_TIMERS == 1 )
;;;2076           {
;;;2077               if( xReturn == pdPASS )
;;;2078               {
;;;2079                   xReturn = xTimerCreateTimerTask();
;;;2080               }
;;;2081               else
;;;2082               {
;;;2083                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2084               }
;;;2085           }
;;;2086       #endif /* configUSE_TIMERS */
;;;2087   
;;;2088       if( xReturn == pdPASS )
;;;2089       {
;;;2090           /* freertos_tasks_c_additions_init() should only be called if the user
;;;2091            * definable macro FREERTOS_TASKS_C_ADDITIONS_INIT() is defined, as that is
;;;2092            * the only macro called by the function. */
;;;2093           #ifdef FREERTOS_TASKS_C_ADDITIONS_INIT
;;;2094               {
;;;2095                   freertos_tasks_c_additions_init();
;;;2096               }
;;;2097           #endif
;;;2098   
;;;2099           /* Interrupts are turned off here, to ensure a tick does not occur
;;;2100            * before or during the call to xPortStartScheduler().  The stacks of
;;;2101            * the created tasks contain a status word with interrupts switched on
;;;2102            * so interrupts will automatically get re-enabled when the first task
;;;2103            * starts to run. */
;;;2104           portDISABLE_INTERRUPTS();
;;;2105   
;;;2106           #if ( configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT == 1 )
;;;2107               {
;;;2108                   /* Switch Newlib's _impure_ptr variable to point to the _reent
;;;2109                    * structure specific to the task that will run first.
;;;2110                    * See the third party link http://www.nadler.com/embedded/newlibAndFreeRTOS.html
;;;2111                    * for additional information. */
;;;2112                   _impure_ptr = &( pxCurrentTCB->xNewLib_reent );
;;;2113               }
;;;2114           #endif /* configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT */
;;;2115   
;;;2116           xNextTaskUnblockTime = portMAX_DELAY;
000016  4c0b              LDR      r4,|L39.68|
000018  3c2c              SUBS     r4,r4,#0x2c
00001a  2801              CMP      r0,#1                 ;2088
00001c  d110              BNE      |L39.64|
00001e  20bf              MOVS     r0,#0xbf              ;2088
000020  f3808811          MSR      BASEPRI,r0            ;2088
000024  f3bf8f4f          DSB                            ;2088
000028  f3bf8f6f          ISB                            ;2088
00002c  f04f30ff          MOV      r0,#0xffffffff
000030  62a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x28]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2117           xSchedulerRunning = pdTRUE;
000032  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000034  6160              STR      r0,[r4,#0x14]  ; xSchedulerRunning
;;;2118           xTickCount = ( TickType_t ) configINITIAL_TICK_COUNT;
000036  60e5              STR      r5,[r4,#0xc]  ; xTickCount
;;;2119   
;;;2120           /* If configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS is defined then the following
;;;2121            * macro must be defined to configure the timer/counter used to generate
;;;2122            * the run time counter time base.   NOTE:  If configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS
;;;2123            * is set to 0 and the following line fails to build then ensure you do not
;;;2124            * have portCONFIGURE_TIMER_FOR_RUN_TIME_STATS() defined in your
;;;2125            * FreeRTOSConfig.h file. */
;;;2126           portCONFIGURE_TIMER_FOR_RUN_TIME_STATS();
000038  4806              LDR      r0,|L39.84|
00003a  6005              STR      r5,[r0,#0]  ; ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
;;;2127   
;;;2128           traceTASK_SWITCHED_IN();
;;;2129   
;;;2130           /* Setting up the timer tick is hardware specific and thus in the
;;;2131            * portable interface. */
;;;2132           if( xPortStartScheduler() != pdFALSE )
00003c  f7fffffe          BL       xPortStartScheduler
                  |L39.64|
;;;2133           {
;;;2134               /* Should not reach here as if the scheduler is running the
;;;2135                * function will not return. */
;;;2136           }
;;;2137           else
;;;2138           {
;;;2139               /* Should only reach here if a task calls xTaskEndScheduler(). */
;;;2140           }
;;;2141       }
;;;2142       else
;;;2143       {
;;;2144           /* This line will only be reached if the kernel could not be started,
;;;2145            * because there was not enough FreeRTOS heap to create the idle task
;;;2146            * or the timer task. */
;;;2147           configASSERT( xReturn != errCOULD_NOT_ALLOCATE_REQUIRED_MEMORY );
;;;2148       }
;;;2149   
;;;2150       /* Prevent compiler warnings if INCLUDE_xTaskGetIdleTaskHandle is set to 0,
;;;2151        * meaning xIdleTaskHandle is not used anywhere else. */
;;;2152       ( void ) xIdleTaskHandle;
;;;2153   
;;;2154       /* OpenOCD makes use of uxTopUsedPriority for thread debugging. Prevent uxTopUsedPriority
;;;2155        * from getting optimized out as it is no longer used by the kernel. */
;;;2156       ( void ) uxTopUsedPriority;
000040  6b20              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x30]  ; uxTopUsedPriority
;;;2157   }
000042  bd7c              POP      {r2-r6,pc}
;;;2158   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L39.68|
                          DCD      ||.data||+0x2c
                  |L39.72|
000048  49444c45          DCB      "IDLE",0
00004c  00      
00004d  00                DCB      0
00004e  00                DCB      0
00004f  00                DCB      0
                  |L39.80|
                          DCD      prvIdleTask
                  |L39.84|
                          DCD      ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSuspend||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSuspend PROC
;;;1750   
;;;1751       void vTaskSuspend( TaskHandle_t xTaskToSuspend )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1752       {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1753           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;1754   
;;;1755           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1756           {
;;;1757               /* If null is passed in here then it is the running task that is
;;;1758                * being suspended. */
;;;1759               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTaskToSuspend );
000008  4d25              LDR      r5,|L40.160|
00000a  b904              CBNZ     r4,|L40.14|
00000c  682c              LDR      r4,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
                  |L40.14|
;;;1760   
;;;1761               traceTASK_SUSPEND( pxTCB );
;;;1762   
;;;1763               /* Remove task from the ready/delayed list and place in the
;;;1764                * suspended list. */
;;;1765               if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
00000e  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000010  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
000014  b958              CBNZ     r0,|L40.46|
;;;1766               {
;;;1767                   taskRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxTCB->uxPriority );
000016  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000018  4a22              LDR      r2,|L40.164|
00001a  eb000180          ADD      r1,r0,r0,LSL #2
00001e  f8521021          LDR      r1,[r2,r1,LSL #2]
000022  b921              CBNZ     r1,|L40.46|
000024  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000026  4081              LSLS     r1,r1,r0
000028  6928              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00002a  4388              BICS     r0,r0,r1
00002c  6128              STR      r0,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L40.46|
;;;1768               }
;;;1769               else
;;;1770               {
;;;1771                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1772               }
;;;1773   
;;;1774               /* Is the task waiting on an event also? */
;;;1775               if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
00002e  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
000030  b118              CBZ      r0,|L40.58|
;;;1776               {
;;;1777                   ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000032  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
000036  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
                  |L40.58|
;;;1778               }
;;;1779               else
;;;1780               {
;;;1781                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1782               }
;;;1783   
;;;1784               vListInsertEnd( &xSuspendedTaskList, &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00003a  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00003c  481a              LDR      r0,|L40.168|
00003e  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;1785   
;;;1786               #if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 )
;;;1787                   {
;;;1788                       BaseType_t x;
;;;1789   
;;;1790                       for( x = 0; x < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES; x++ )
000042  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000044  4606              MOV      r6,r0
                  |L40.70|
;;;1791                       {
;;;1792                           if( pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ x ] == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
000046  1821              ADDS     r1,r4,r0
000048  f891205c          LDRB     r2,[r1,#0x5c]
00004c  2a01              CMP      r2,#1
00004e  d101              BNE      |L40.84|
;;;1793                           {
;;;1794                               /* The task was blocked to wait for a notification, but is
;;;1795                                * now suspended, so no notification was received. */
;;;1796                               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ x ] = taskNOT_WAITING_NOTIFICATION;
000050  f881605c          STRB     r6,[r1,#0x5c]
                  |L40.84|
000054  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000056  2801              CMP      r0,#1                 ;1790
000058  dbf5              BLT      |L40.70|
;;;1797                           }
;;;1798                       }
;;;1799                   }
;;;1800               #endif /* if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 ) */
;;;1801           }
;;;1802           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00005a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1803   
;;;1804           if( xSchedulerRunning != pdFALSE )
00005e  6968              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x14]  ; xSchedulerRunning
000060  b128              CBZ      r0,|L40.110|
;;;1805           {
;;;1806               /* Reset the next expected unblock time in case it referred to the
;;;1807                * task that is now in the Suspended state. */
;;;1808               taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000062  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1809               {
;;;1810                   prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
000066  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;1811               }
;;;1812               taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00006a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
                  |L40.110|
;;;1813           }
;;;1814           else
;;;1815           {
;;;1816               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1817           }
;;;1818   
;;;1819           if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
00006e  6828              LDR      r0,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000070  4284              CMP      r4,r0
000072  d109              BNE      |L40.136|
;;;1820           {
;;;1821               if( xSchedulerRunning != pdFALSE )
000074  6968              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x14]  ; xSchedulerRunning
000076  b140              CBZ      r0,|L40.138|
;;;1822               {
;;;1823                   /* The current task has just been suspended. */
;;;1824                   configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1825                   portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
000078  490c              LDR      r1,|L40.172|
00007a  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
00007e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000080  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000084  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L40.136|
;;;1826               }
;;;1827               else
;;;1828               {
;;;1829                   /* The scheduler is not running, but the task that was pointed
;;;1830                    * to by pxCurrentTCB has just been suspended and pxCurrentTCB
;;;1831                    * must be adjusted to point to a different task. */
;;;1832                   if( listCURRENT_LIST_LENGTH( &xSuspendedTaskList ) == uxCurrentNumberOfTasks ) /*lint !e931 Right has no side effect, just volatile. */
;;;1833                   {
;;;1834                       /* No other tasks are ready, so set pxCurrentTCB back to
;;;1835                        * NULL so when the next task is created pxCurrentTCB will
;;;1836                        * be set to point to it no matter what its relative priority
;;;1837                        * is. */
;;;1838                       pxCurrentTCB = NULL;
;;;1839                   }
;;;1840                   else
;;;1841                   {
;;;1842                       vTaskSwitchContext();
;;;1843                   }
;;;1844               }
;;;1845           }
;;;1846           else
;;;1847           {
;;;1848               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1849           }
;;;1850       }
000088  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
                  |L40.138|
00008a  4807              LDR      r0,|L40.168|
00008c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]            ;1832  ; xSuspendedTaskList
00008e  68a9              LDR      r1,[r5,#8]            ;1832  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000090  4288              CMP      r0,r1                 ;1832
000092  d101              BNE      |L40.152|
000094  602e              STR      r6,[r5,#0]            ;1838  ; pxCurrentTCB
000096  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
                  |L40.152|
000098  e8bd4070          POP      {r4-r6,lr}            ;1842
00009c  f7ffbffe          B.W      vTaskSwitchContext
;;;1851   
                          ENDP

                  |L40.160|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L40.164|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L40.168|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x17c
                  |L40.172|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSuspendAll||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSuspendAll PROC
;;;2170   
;;;2171   void vTaskSuspendAll( void )
000000  4802              LDR      r0,|L41.12|
;;;2172   {
;;;2173       /* A critical section is not required as the variable is of type
;;;2174        * BaseType_t.  Please read Richard Barry's reply in the following link to a
;;;2175        * post in the FreeRTOS support forum before reporting this as a bug! -
;;;2176        * https://goo.gl/wu4acr */
;;;2177   
;;;2178       /* portSOFRWARE_BARRIER() is only implemented for emulated/simulated ports that
;;;2179        * do not otherwise exhibit real time behaviour. */
;;;2180       portSOFTWARE_BARRIER();
;;;2181   
;;;2182       /* The scheduler is suspended if uxSchedulerSuspended is non-zero.  An increment
;;;2183        * is used to allow calls to vTaskSuspendAll() to nest. */
;;;2184       ++uxSchedulerSuspended;
000002  6b41              LDR      r1,[r0,#0x34]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000004  1c49              ADDS     r1,r1,#1
000006  6341              STR      r1,[r0,#0x34]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
;;;2185   
;;;2186       /* Enforces ordering for ports and optimised compilers that may otherwise place
;;;2187        * the above increment elsewhere. */
;;;2188       portMEMORY_BARRIER();
;;;2189   }
000008  4770              BX       lr
;;;2190   /*----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

00000a  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L41.12|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSwitchContext||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSwitchContext PROC
;;;3054   
;;;3055   void vTaskSwitchContext( void )
000000  4a16              LDR      r2,|L42.92|
;;;3056   {
000002  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;3057       if( uxSchedulerSuspended != ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000004  6b50              LDR      r0,[r2,#0x34]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000006  b110              CBZ      r0,|L42.14|
;;;3058       {
;;;3059           /* The scheduler is currently suspended - do not allow a context
;;;3060            * switch. */
;;;3061           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000008  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00000a  61d0              STR      r0,[r2,#0x1c]  ; xYieldPending
;;;3062       }
;;;3063       else
;;;3064       {
;;;3065           xYieldPending = pdFALSE;
;;;3066           traceTASK_SWITCHED_OUT();
;;;3067   
;;;3068           #if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 )
;;;3069               {
;;;3070                   #ifdef portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE
;;;3071                       portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE( ulTotalRunTime );
;;;3072                   #else
;;;3073                       ulTotalRunTime = portGET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE();
;;;3074                   #endif
;;;3075   
;;;3076                   /* Add the amount of time the task has been running to the
;;;3077                    * accumulated time so far.  The time the task started running was
;;;3078                    * stored in ulTaskSwitchedInTime.  Note that there is no overflow
;;;3079                    * protection here so count values are only valid until the timer
;;;3080                    * overflows.  The guard against negative values is to protect
;;;3081                    * against suspect run time stat counter implementations - which
;;;3082                    * are provided by the application, not the kernel. */
;;;3083                   if( ulTotalRunTime > ulTaskSwitchedInTime )
;;;3084                   {
;;;3085                       pxCurrentTCB->ulRunTimeCounter += ( ulTotalRunTime - ulTaskSwitchedInTime );
;;;3086                   }
;;;3087                   else
;;;3088                   {
;;;3089                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;3090                   }
;;;3091   
;;;3092                   ulTaskSwitchedInTime = ulTotalRunTime;
;;;3093               }
;;;3094           #endif /* configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS */
;;;3095   
;;;3096           /* Check for stack overflow, if configured. */
;;;3097           taskCHECK_FOR_STACK_OVERFLOW();
;;;3098   
;;;3099           /* Before the currently running task is switched out, save its errno. */
;;;3100           #if ( configUSE_POSIX_ERRNO == 1 )
;;;3101               {
;;;3102                   pxCurrentTCB->iTaskErrno = FreeRTOS_errno;
;;;3103               }
;;;3104           #endif
;;;3105   
;;;3106           /* Select a new task to run using either the generic C or port
;;;3107            * optimised asm code. */
;;;3108           taskSELECT_HIGHEST_PRIORITY_TASK(); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
;;;3109           traceTASK_SWITCHED_IN();
;;;3110   
;;;3111           /* After the new task is switched in, update the global errno. */
;;;3112           #if ( configUSE_POSIX_ERRNO == 1 )
;;;3113               {
;;;3114                   FreeRTOS_errno = pxCurrentTCB->iTaskErrno;
;;;3115               }
;;;3116           #endif
;;;3117   
;;;3118           #if ( configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT == 1 )
;;;3119               {
;;;3120                   /* Switch Newlib's _impure_ptr variable to point to the _reent
;;;3121                    * structure specific to this task.
;;;3122                    * See the third party link http://www.nadler.com/embedded/newlibAndFreeRTOS.html
;;;3123                    * for additional information. */
;;;3124                   _impure_ptr = &( pxCurrentTCB->xNewLib_reent );
;;;3125               }
;;;3126           #endif /* configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT */
;;;3127       }
;;;3128   }
00000c  bd10              POP      {r4,pc}
                  |L42.14|
00000e  2000              MOVS     r0,#0                 ;3065
000010  61d0              STR      r0,[r2,#0x1c]         ;3065  ; xYieldPending
000012  4813              LDR      r0,|L42.96|
000014  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]            ;3073  ; ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
000016  63d0              STR      r0,[r2,#0x3c]         ;3073  ; ulTotalRunTime
000018  6bd0              LDR      r0,[r2,#0x3c]         ;3083  ; ulTotalRunTime
00001a  6b91              LDR      r1,[r2,#0x38]         ;3083  ; ulTaskSwitchedInTime
00001c  4288              CMP      r0,r1                 ;3083
00001e  d905              BLS      |L42.44|
000020  6810              LDR      r0,[r2,#0]            ;3085  ; pxCurrentTCB
000022  6bd4              LDR      r4,[r2,#0x3c]         ;3085  ; ulTotalRunTime
000024  6d43              LDR      r3,[r0,#0x54]         ;3085
000026  1a61              SUBS     r1,r4,r1              ;3085
000028  4419              ADD      r1,r1,r3              ;3085
00002a  6541              STR      r1,[r0,#0x54]         ;3085
                  |L42.44|
00002c  6bd0              LDR      r0,[r2,#0x3c]         ;3092  ; ulTotalRunTime
00002e  6390              STR      r0,[r2,#0x38]         ;3108  ; ulTaskSwitchedInTime
000030  6910              LDR      r0,[r2,#0x10]         ;3108  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000032  490c              LDR      r1,|L42.100|
000034  fab0f080          CLZ      r0,r0                 ;3108
000038  f1c0001f          RSB      r0,r0,#0x1f           ;3108
00003c  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2       ;3108
000040  eb010080          ADD      r0,r1,r0,LSL #2       ;3108
000044  f1000308          ADD      r3,r0,#8              ;3108
000048  6841              LDR      r1,[r0,#4]            ;3108
00004a  6849              LDR      r1,[r1,#4]            ;3108
00004c  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]            ;3108
00004e  4299              CMP      r1,r3                 ;3108
000050  d101              BNE      |L42.86|
000052  6849              LDR      r1,[r1,#4]            ;3108
000054  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]            ;3108
                  |L42.86|
000056  68c8              LDR      r0,[r1,#0xc]          ;3108
000058  6010              STR      r0,[r2,#0]            ;3108  ; pxCurrentTCB
00005a  bd10              POP      {r4,pc}
;;;3129   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L42.92|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L42.96|
                          DCD      ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
                  |L42.100|
                          DCD      ||.bss||

                          AREA ||i.xTaskCatchUpTicks||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskCatchUpTicks PROC
;;;2671   
;;;2672   BaseType_t xTaskCatchUpTicks( TickType_t xTicksToCatchUp )
000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;2673   {
000002  4602              MOV      r2,r0
;;;2674       BaseType_t xYieldOccurred;
;;;2675   
;;;2676       /* Must not be called with the scheduler suspended as the implementation
;;;2677        * relies on xPendedTicks being wound down to 0 in xTaskResumeAll(). */
;;;2678       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;2679   
;;;2680       /* Use xPendedTicks to mimic xTicksToCatchUp number of ticks occurring when
;;;2681        * the scheduler is suspended so the ticks are executed in xTaskResumeAll(). */
;;;2682       vTaskSuspendAll();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;2683       xPendedTicks += xTicksToCatchUp;
000008  4903              LDR      r1,|L43.24|
00000a  698b              LDR      r3,[r1,#0x18]  ; xPendedTicks
00000c  1898              ADDS     r0,r3,r2
00000e  6188              STR      r0,[r1,#0x18]  ; xPendedTicks
;;;2684       xYieldOccurred = xTaskResumeAll();
000010  e8bd4010          POP      {r4,lr}
000014  f7ffbffe          B.W      xTaskResumeAll
;;;2685   
;;;2686       return xYieldOccurred;
;;;2687   }
;;;2688   /*----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L43.24|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.xTaskCheckForTimeOut||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskCheckForTimeOut PROC
;;;3346   
;;;3347   BaseType_t xTaskCheckForTimeOut( TimeOut_t * const pxTimeOut,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;3348                                    TickType_t * const pxTicksToWait )
;;;3349   {
000004  460d              MOV      r5,r1
000006  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;3350       BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;3351   
;;;3352       configASSERT( pxTimeOut );
;;;3353       configASSERT( pxTicksToWait );
;;;3354   
;;;3355       taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000008  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;3356       {
;;;3357           /* Minor optimisation.  The tick count cannot change in this block. */
;;;3358           const TickType_t xConstTickCount = xTickCount;
00000c  4e11              LDR      r6,|L44.84|
00000e  68f2              LDR      r2,[r6,#0xc]  ; xTickCount
;;;3359           const TickType_t xElapsedTime = xConstTickCount - pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering;
000010  6863              LDR      r3,[r4,#4]
;;;3360   
;;;3361           #if ( INCLUDE_xTaskAbortDelay == 1 )
;;;3362               if( pxCurrentTCB->ucDelayAborted != ( uint8_t ) pdFALSE )
;;;3363               {
;;;3364                   /* The delay was aborted, which is not the same as a time out,
;;;3365                    * but has the same result. */
;;;3366                   pxCurrentTCB->ucDelayAborted = pdFALSE;
;;;3367                   xReturn = pdTRUE;
;;;3368               }
;;;3369               else
;;;3370           #endif
;;;3371   
;;;3372           #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;3373               if( *pxTicksToWait == portMAX_DELAY )
000012  6828              LDR      r0,[r5,#0]
000014  1ad1              SUBS     r1,r2,r3              ;3359
000016  1c47              ADDS     r7,r0,#1
000018  d00a              BEQ      |L44.48|
;;;3374               {
;;;3375                   /* If INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend is set to 1 and the block time
;;;3376                    * specified is the maximum block time then the task should block
;;;3377                    * indefinitely, and therefore never time out. */
;;;3378                   xReturn = pdFALSE;
;;;3379               }
;;;3380               else
;;;3381           #endif
;;;3382   
;;;3383           if( ( xNumOfOverflows != pxTimeOut->xOverflowCount ) && ( xConstTickCount >= pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering ) ) /*lint !e525 Indentation preferred as is to make code within pre-processor directives clearer. */
00001a  6827              LDR      r7,[r4,#0]
00001c  f8d6c020          LDR      r12,[r6,#0x20]  ; xNumOfOverflows
000020  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
000022  4567              CMP      r7,r12
000024  d006              BEQ      |L44.52|
000026  4293              CMP      r3,r2
000028  d804              BHI      |L44.52|
;;;3384           {
;;;3385               /* The tick count is greater than the time at which
;;;3386                * vTaskSetTimeout() was called, but has also overflowed since
;;;3387                * vTaskSetTimeOut() was called.  It must have wrapped all the way
;;;3388                * around and gone past again. This passed since vTaskSetTimeout()
;;;3389                * was called. */
;;;3390               xReturn = pdTRUE;
00002a  2401              MOVS     r4,#1
;;;3391               *pxTicksToWait = ( TickType_t ) 0;
00002c  602e              STR      r6,[r5,#0]
00002e  e00b              B        |L44.72|
                  |L44.48|
000030  2400              MOVS     r4,#0                 ;3378
000032  e009              B        |L44.72|
                  |L44.52|
;;;3392           }
;;;3393           else if( xElapsedTime < *pxTicksToWait ) /*lint !e961 Explicit casting is only redundant with some compilers, whereas others require it to prevent integer conversion errors. */
000034  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000036  d905              BLS      |L44.68|
;;;3394           {
;;;3395               /* Not a genuine timeout. Adjust parameters for time remaining. */
;;;3396               *pxTicksToWait -= xElapsedTime;
000038  1a40              SUBS     r0,r0,r1
;;;3397               vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState( pxTimeOut );
00003a  6028              STR      r0,[r5,#0]
00003c  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00003e  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState
;;;3398               xReturn = pdFALSE;
000042  e7f5              B        |L44.48|
                  |L44.68|
;;;3399           }
;;;3400           else
;;;3401           {
;;;3402               *pxTicksToWait = ( TickType_t ) 0;
;;;3403               xReturn = pdTRUE;
000044  2401              MOVS     r4,#1
000046  602e              STR      r6,[r5,#0]
                  |L44.72|
;;;3404           }
;;;3405       }
;;;3406       taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000048  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;3407   
;;;3408       return xReturn;
00004c  4620              MOV      r0,r4
;;;3409   }
00004e  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;3410   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000052  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L44.84|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.xTaskCreate||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  xTaskCreate PROC
;;;770    
;;;771        BaseType_t xTaskCreate( TaskFunction_t pxTaskCode,
000000  e92d5fff          PUSH     {r0-r12,lr}
;;;772                                const char * const pcName, /*lint !e971 Unqualified char types are allowed for strings and single characters only. */
;;;773                                const configSTACK_DEPTH_TYPE usStackDepth,
;;;774                                void * const pvParameters,
;;;775                                UBaseType_t uxPriority,
;;;776                                TaskHandle_t * const pxCreatedTask )
;;;777        {
000004  4683              MOV      r11,r0
000006  e9dd870e          LDRD     r8,r7,[sp,#0x38]
00000a  4699              MOV      r9,r3
00000c  4616              MOV      r6,r2
00000e  468a              MOV      r10,r1
;;;778            TCB_t * pxNewTCB;
;;;779            BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;780    
;;;781            /* If the stack grows down then allocate the stack then the TCB so the stack
;;;782             * does not grow into the TCB.  Likewise if the stack grows up then allocate
;;;783             * the TCB then the stack. */
;;;784            #if ( portSTACK_GROWTH > 0 )
;;;785                {
;;;786                    /* Allocate space for the TCB.  Where the memory comes from depends on
;;;787                     * the implementation of the port malloc function and whether or not static
;;;788                     * allocation is being used. */
;;;789                    pxNewTCB = ( TCB_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( TCB_t ) );
;;;790    
;;;791                    if( pxNewTCB != NULL )
;;;792                    {
;;;793                        /* Allocate space for the stack used by the task being created.
;;;794                         * The base of the stack memory stored in the TCB so the task can
;;;795                         * be deleted later if required. */
;;;796                        pxNewTCB->pxStack = ( StackType_t * ) pvPortMalloc( ( ( ( size_t ) usStackDepth ) * sizeof( StackType_t ) ) ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
;;;797    
;;;798                        if( pxNewTCB->pxStack == NULL )
;;;799                        {
;;;800                            /* Could not allocate the stack.  Delete the allocated TCB. */
;;;801                            vPortFree( pxNewTCB );
;;;802                            pxNewTCB = NULL;
;;;803                        }
;;;804                    }
;;;805                }
;;;806            #else /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;807                {
;;;808                    StackType_t * pxStack;
;;;809    
;;;810                    /* Allocate space for the stack used by the task being created. */
;;;811                    pxStack = pvPortMalloc( ( ( ( size_t ) usStackDepth ) * sizeof( StackType_t ) ) ); /*lint !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack and this allocation is the stack. */
000010  0090              LSLS     r0,r2,#2
000012  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
000016  0005              MOVS     r5,r0
;;;812    
;;;813                    if( pxStack != NULL )
000018  d01a              BEQ      |L45.80|
;;;814                    {
;;;815                        /* Allocate space for the TCB. */
;;;816                        pxNewTCB = ( TCB_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( TCB_t ) ); /*lint !e9087 !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack, and the first member of TCB_t is always a pointer to the task's stack. */
00001a  2060              MOVS     r0,#0x60
00001c  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
000020  0004              MOVS     r4,r0
;;;817    
;;;818                        if( pxNewTCB != NULL )
000022  d012              BEQ      |L45.74|
;;;819                        {
;;;820                            /* Store the stack location in the TCB. */
;;;821                            pxNewTCB->pxStack = pxStack;
;;;822                        }
;;;823                        else
;;;824                        {
;;;825                            /* The stack cannot be used as the TCB was not created.  Free
;;;826                             * it again. */
;;;827                            vPortFree( pxStack );
;;;828                        }
;;;829                    }
;;;830                    else
;;;831                    {
;;;832                        pxNewTCB = NULL;
;;;833                    }
;;;834                }
;;;835            #endif /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;836    
;;;837            if( pxNewTCB != NULL )
;;;838            {
;;;839                #if ( tskSTATIC_AND_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION_POSSIBLE != 0 ) /*lint !e9029 !e731 Macro has been consolidated for readability reasons. */
;;;840                    {
;;;841                        /* Tasks can be created statically or dynamically, so note this
;;;842                         * task was created dynamically in case it is later deleted. */
;;;843                        pxNewTCB->ucStaticallyAllocated = tskDYNAMICALLY_ALLOCATED_STACK_AND_TCB;
;;;844                    }
;;;845                #endif /* tskSTATIC_AND_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION_POSSIBLE */
;;;846    
;;;847                prvInitialiseNewTask( pxTaskCode, pcName, ( uint32_t ) usStackDepth, pvParameters, uxPriority, pxCreatedTask, pxNewTCB, NULL );
000024  6325              STR      r5,[r4,#0x30]
000026  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
000028  e9cd4102          STRD     r4,r1,[sp,#8]
00002c  e9cd8700          STRD     r8,r7,[sp,#0]
000030  464b              MOV      r3,r9
000032  4632              MOV      r2,r6
000034  4651              MOV      r1,r10
000036  4658              MOV      r0,r11
000038  f7fffffe          BL       prvInitialiseNewTask
;;;848                prvAddNewTaskToReadyList( pxNewTCB );
00003c  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00003e  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddNewTaskToReadyList
;;;849                xReturn = pdPASS;
000042  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
                  |L45.68|
;;;850            }
;;;851            else
;;;852            {
;;;853                xReturn = errCOULD_NOT_ALLOCATE_REQUIRED_MEMORY;
;;;854            }
;;;855    
;;;856            return xReturn;
;;;857        }
000044  b004              ADD      sp,sp,#0x10
000046  e8bd9ff0          POP      {r4-r12,pc}
                  |L45.74|
00004a  4628              MOV      r0,r5                 ;827
00004c  f7fffffe          BL       vPortFree
                  |L45.80|
000050  f04f30ff          MOV      r0,#0xffffffff        ;853
000054  e7f6              B        |L45.68|
;;;858    
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.xTaskDelayUntil||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskDelayUntil PROC
;;;1293   
;;;1294       BaseType_t xTaskDelayUntil( TickType_t * const pxPreviousWakeTime,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1295                                   const TickType_t xTimeIncrement )
;;;1296       {
000002  460d              MOV      r5,r1
000004  4603              MOV      r3,r0
;;;1297           TickType_t xTimeToWake;
;;;1298           BaseType_t xAlreadyYielded, xShouldDelay = pdFALSE;
000006  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
;;;1299   
;;;1300           configASSERT( pxPreviousWakeTime );
;;;1301           configASSERT( ( xTimeIncrement > 0U ) );
;;;1302           configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1303   
;;;1304           vTaskSuspendAll();
000008  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;1305           {
;;;1306               /* Minor optimisation.  The tick count cannot change in this
;;;1307                * block. */
;;;1308               const TickType_t xConstTickCount = xTickCount;
00000c  4810              LDR      r0,|L46.80|
00000e  68c2              LDR      r2,[r0,#0xc]  ; xTickCount
;;;1309   
;;;1310               /* Generate the tick time at which the task wants to wake. */
;;;1311               xTimeToWake = *pxPreviousWakeTime + xTimeIncrement;
000010  6818              LDR      r0,[r3,#0]
000012  1941              ADDS     r1,r0,r5
;;;1312   
;;;1313               if( xConstTickCount < *pxPreviousWakeTime )
000014  4290              CMP      r0,r2
000016  d902              BLS      |L46.30|
;;;1314               {
;;;1315                   /* The tick count has overflowed since this function was
;;;1316                    * lasted called.  In this case the only time we should ever
;;;1317                    * actually delay is if the wake time has also  overflowed,
;;;1318                    * and the wake time is greater than the tick time.  When this
;;;1319                    * is the case it is as if neither time had overflowed. */
;;;1320                   if( ( xTimeToWake < *pxPreviousWakeTime ) && ( xTimeToWake > xConstTickCount ) )
000018  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00001a  d802              BHI      |L46.34|
00001c  e004              B        |L46.40|
                  |L46.30|
;;;1321                   {
;;;1322                       xShouldDelay = pdTRUE;
;;;1323                   }
;;;1324                   else
;;;1325                   {
;;;1326                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1327                   }
;;;1328               }
;;;1329               else
;;;1330               {
;;;1331                   /* The tick time has not overflowed.  In this case we will
;;;1332                    * delay if either the wake time has overflowed, and/or the
;;;1333                    * tick time is less than the wake time. */
;;;1334                   if( ( xTimeToWake < *pxPreviousWakeTime ) || ( xTimeToWake > xConstTickCount ) )
00001e  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000020  d801              BHI      |L46.38|
                  |L46.34|
000022  4291              CMP      r1,r2
000024  d900              BLS      |L46.40|
                  |L46.38|
;;;1335                   {
;;;1336                       xShouldDelay = pdTRUE;
000026  2401              MOVS     r4,#1
                  |L46.40|
;;;1337                   }
;;;1338                   else
;;;1339                   {
;;;1340                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1341                   }
;;;1342               }
;;;1343   
;;;1344               /* Update the wake time ready for the next call. */
;;;1345               *pxPreviousWakeTime = xTimeToWake;
;;;1346   
;;;1347               if( xShouldDelay != pdFALSE )
000028  6019              STR      r1,[r3,#0]
00002a  b11c              CBZ      r4,|L46.52|
;;;1348               {
;;;1349                   traceTASK_DELAY_UNTIL( xTimeToWake );
;;;1350   
;;;1351                   /* prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList() needs the block time, not
;;;1352                    * the time to wake, so subtract the current tick count. */
;;;1353                   prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTimeToWake - xConstTickCount, pdFALSE );
00002c  1a88              SUBS     r0,r1,r2
00002e  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
000030  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
                  |L46.52|
;;;1354               }
;;;1355               else
;;;1356               {
;;;1357                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1358               }
;;;1359           }
;;;1360           xAlreadyYielded = xTaskResumeAll();
000034  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
;;;1361   
;;;1362           /* Force a reschedule if xTaskResumeAll has not already done so, we may
;;;1363            * have put ourselves to sleep. */
;;;1364           if( xAlreadyYielded == pdFALSE )
000038  b938              CBNZ     r0,|L46.74|
;;;1365           {
;;;1366               portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
00003a  4906              LDR      r1,|L46.84|
00003c  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000040  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000042  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000046  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L46.74|
;;;1367           }
;;;1368           else
;;;1369           {
;;;1370               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1371           }
;;;1372   
;;;1373           return xShouldDelay;
00004a  4620              MOV      r0,r4
;;;1374       }
00004c  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1375   
                          ENDP

00004e  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L46.80|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L46.84|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotify||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotify PROC
;;;4886   
;;;4887       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotify( TaskHandle_t xTaskToNotify,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;4888                                      UBaseType_t uxIndexToNotify,
;;;4889                                      uint32_t ulValue,
;;;4890                                      eNotifyAction eAction,
;;;4891                                      uint32_t * pulPreviousNotificationValue )
;;;4892       {
000004  4699              MOV      r9,r3
000006  4615              MOV      r5,r2
000008  460f              MOV      r7,r1
00000a  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;4893           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;4894           BaseType_t xReturn = pdPASS;
00000c  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
;;;4895           uint8_t ucOriginalNotifyState;
;;;4896   
;;;4897           configASSERT( uxIndexToNotify < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;4898           configASSERT( xTaskToNotify );
;;;4899           pxTCB = xTaskToNotify;
;;;4900   
;;;4901           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
00000e  f8dd8020          LDR      r8,[sp,#0x20]
000012  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;4902           {
;;;4903               if( pulPreviousNotificationValue != NULL )
;;;4904               {
;;;4905                   *pulPreviousNotificationValue = pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ];
000016  eb040087          ADD      r0,r4,r7,LSL #2
00001a  f1b80f00          CMP      r8,#0                 ;4903
00001e  d002              BEQ      |L47.38|
000020  6d82              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x58]
000022  f8c82000          STR      r2,[r8,#0]
                  |L47.38|
;;;4906               }
;;;4907   
;;;4908               ucOriginalNotifyState = pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ];
000026  19e2              ADDS     r2,r4,r7
000028  f8121f5c          LDRB     r1,[r2,#0x5c]!
;;;4909   
;;;4910               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ] = taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED;
00002c  2302              MOVS     r3,#2
00002e  7013              STRB     r3,[r2,#0]
;;;4911   
;;;4912               switch( eAction )
000030  f1b90f05          CMP      r9,#5
000034  d20e              BCS      |L47.84|
000036  e8dff009          TBB      [pc,r9]
00003a  0d03              DCB      0x0d,0x03
00003c  060c0a00          DCB      0x06,0x0c,0x0a,0x00
;;;4913               {
;;;4914                   case eSetBits:
;;;4915                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] |= ulValue;
000040  6d82              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x58]
000042  432a              ORRS     r2,r2,r5
;;;4916                       break;
000044  e001              B        |L47.74|
;;;4917   
;;;4918                   case eIncrement:
;;;4919                       ( pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] )++;
000046  6d82              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x58]
000048  1c52              ADDS     r2,r2,#1
                  |L47.74|
00004a  6582              STR      r2,[r0,#0x58]         ;4915
00004c  e002              B        |L47.84|
;;;4920                       break;
;;;4921   
;;;4922                   case eSetValueWithOverwrite:
;;;4923                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
;;;4924                       break;
;;;4925   
;;;4926                   case eSetValueWithoutOverwrite:
;;;4927   
;;;4928                       if( ucOriginalNotifyState != taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
00004e  2902              CMP      r1,#2
000050  d003              BEQ      |L47.90|
;;;4929                       {
;;;4930                           pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
000052  6585              STR      r5,[r0,#0x58]
                  |L47.84|
;;;4931                       }
;;;4932                       else
;;;4933                       {
;;;4934                           /* The value could not be written to the task. */
;;;4935                           xReturn = pdFAIL;
;;;4936                       }
;;;4937   
;;;4938                       break;
;;;4939   
;;;4940                   case eNoAction:
;;;4941   
;;;4942                       /* The task is being notified without its notify value being
;;;4943                        * updated. */
;;;4944                       break;
;;;4945   
;;;4946                   default:
;;;4947   
;;;4948                       /* Should not get here if all enums are handled.
;;;4949                        * Artificially force an assert by testing a value the
;;;4950                        * compiler can't assume is const. */
;;;4951                       configASSERT( xTickCount == ( TickType_t ) 0 );
;;;4952   
;;;4953                       break;
;;;4954               }
;;;4955   
;;;4956               traceTASK_NOTIFY( uxIndexToNotify );
;;;4957   
;;;4958               /* If the task is in the blocked state specifically to wait for a
;;;4959                * notification then unblock it now. */
;;;4960               if( ucOriginalNotifyState == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
000054  2901              CMP      r1,#1
000056  d002              BEQ      |L47.94|
000058  e020              B        |L47.156|
                  |L47.90|
00005a  2600              MOVS     r6,#0                 ;4935
00005c  e01e              B        |L47.156|
                  |L47.94|
;;;4961               {
;;;4962                   ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00005e  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000060  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;4963                   prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000064  4d10              LDR      r5,|L47.168|
000066  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000068  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
00006a  692a              LDR      r2,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00006c  4081              LSLS     r1,r1,r0
00006e  4311              ORRS     r1,r1,r2
000070  6129              STR      r1,[r5,#0x10]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000072  490e              LDR      r1,|L47.172|
000074  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
000078  eb010080          ADD      r0,r1,r0,LSL #2
00007c  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00007e  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;4964   
;;;4965                   /* The task should not have been on an event list. */
;;;4966                   configASSERT( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == NULL );
;;;4967   
;;;4968                   #if ( configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE != 0 )
;;;4969                       {
;;;4970                           /* If a task is blocked waiting for a notification then
;;;4971                            * xNextTaskUnblockTime might be set to the blocked task's time
;;;4972                            * out time.  If the task is unblocked for a reason other than
;;;4973                            * a timeout xNextTaskUnblockTime is normally left unchanged,
;;;4974                            * because it will automatically get reset to a new value when
;;;4975                            * the tick count equals xNextTaskUnblockTime.  However if
;;;4976                            * tickless idling is used it might be more important to enter
;;;4977                            * sleep mode at the earliest possible time - so reset
;;;4978                            * xNextTaskUnblockTime here to ensure it is updated at the
;;;4979                            * earliest possible time. */
;;;4980                           prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
;;;4981                       }
;;;4982                   #endif
;;;4983   
;;;4984                   if( pxTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000082  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000084  6829              LDR      r1,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000086  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000088  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00008a  d907              BLS      |L47.156|
;;;4985                   {
;;;4986                       /* The notified task has a priority above the currently
;;;4987                        * executing task so a yield is required. */
;;;4988                       taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
00008c  4908              LDR      r1,|L47.176|
00008e  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000092  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000094  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000098  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L47.156|
;;;4989                   }
;;;4990                   else
;;;4991                   {
;;;4992                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4993                   }
;;;4994               }
;;;4995               else
;;;4996               {
;;;4997                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4998               }
;;;4999           }
;;;5000           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00009c  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;5001   
;;;5002           return xReturn;
0000a0  4630              MOV      r0,r6
;;;5003       }
0000a2  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;5004   
                          ENDP

0000a6  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L47.168|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L47.172|
                          DCD      ||.bss||
                  |L47.176|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotifyFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotifyFromISR PROC
;;;5009   
;;;5010       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotifyFromISR( TaskHandle_t xTaskToNotify,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;5011                                             UBaseType_t uxIndexToNotify,
;;;5012                                             uint32_t ulValue,
;;;5013                                             eNotifyAction eAction,
;;;5014                                             uint32_t * pulPreviousNotificationValue,
;;;5015                                             BaseType_t * pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken )
;;;5016       {
;;;5017           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;5018           uint8_t ucOriginalNotifyState;
;;;5019           BaseType_t xReturn = pdPASS;
000004  f04f0801          MOV      r8,#1
000008  e9dd6908          LDRD     r6,r9,[sp,#0x20]      ;5016
;;;5020           UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;5021   
;;;5022           configASSERT( xTaskToNotify );
;;;5023           configASSERT( uxIndexToNotify < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;5024   
;;;5025           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;5026            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;5027            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;5028            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;5029            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;5030            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;5031            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;5032            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;5033            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;5034            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;5035            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;5036            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;5037            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;5038            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;5039            * provided on the following link:
;;;5040            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;5041           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;5042   
;;;5043           pxTCB = xTaskToNotify;
00000c  4604              MOV      r4,r0
00000e  27bf              MOVS     r7,#0xbf
000010  f3ef8511          MRS      r5,BASEPRI
000014  f3878811          MSR      BASEPRI,r7
000018  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00001c  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
;;;5044   
;;;5045           uxSavedInterruptStatus = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
;;;5046           {
;;;5047               if( pulPreviousNotificationValue != NULL )
;;;5048               {
;;;5049                   *pulPreviousNotificationValue = pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ];
000020  eb040081          ADD      r0,r4,r1,LSL #2
000024  b10e              CBZ      r6,|L48.42|
000026  6d87              LDR      r7,[r0,#0x58]
000028  6037              STR      r7,[r6,#0]
                  |L48.42|
;;;5050               }
;;;5051   
;;;5052               ucOriginalNotifyState = pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ];
00002a  1866              ADDS     r6,r4,r1
00002c  f8161f5c          LDRB     r1,[r6,#0x5c]!
;;;5053               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ] = taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED;
000030  2702              MOVS     r7,#2
000032  7037              STRB     r7,[r6,#0]
;;;5054   
;;;5055               switch( eAction )
000034  2b05              CMP      r3,#5
000036  d20e              BCS      |L48.86|
000038  e8dff003          TBB      [pc,r3]
00003c  0d03070c          DCB      0x0d,0x03,0x07,0x0c
000040  0a00              DCB      0x0a,0x00
;;;5056               {
;;;5057                   case eSetBits:
;;;5058                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] |= ulValue;
000042  6d83              LDR      r3,[r0,#0x58]
000044  4313              ORRS     r3,r3,r2
000046  6583              STR      r3,[r0,#0x58]
;;;5059                       break;
000048  e005              B        |L48.86|
;;;5060   
;;;5061                   case eIncrement:
;;;5062                       ( pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] )++;
00004a  6d82              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x58]
00004c  1c52              ADDS     r2,r2,#1
;;;5063                       break;
00004e  e001              B        |L48.84|
;;;5064   
;;;5065                   case eSetValueWithOverwrite:
;;;5066                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
;;;5067                       break;
;;;5068   
;;;5069                   case eSetValueWithoutOverwrite:
;;;5070   
;;;5071                       if( ucOriginalNotifyState != taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
000050  2902              CMP      r1,#2
000052  d003              BEQ      |L48.92|
                  |L48.84|
;;;5072                       {
;;;5073                           pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
000054  6582              STR      r2,[r0,#0x58]
                  |L48.86|
;;;5074                       }
;;;5075                       else
;;;5076                       {
;;;5077                           /* The value could not be written to the task. */
;;;5078                           xReturn = pdFAIL;
;;;5079                       }
;;;5080   
;;;5081                       break;
;;;5082   
;;;5083                   case eNoAction:
;;;5084   
;;;5085                       /* The task is being notified without its notify value being
;;;5086                        * updated. */
;;;5087                       break;
;;;5088   
;;;5089                   default:
;;;5090   
;;;5091                       /* Should not get here if all enums are handled.
;;;5092                        * Artificially force an assert by testing a value the
;;;5093                        * compiler can't assume is const. */
;;;5094                       configASSERT( xTickCount == ( TickType_t ) 0 );
;;;5095                       break;
;;;5096               }
;;;5097   
;;;5098               traceTASK_NOTIFY_FROM_ISR( uxIndexToNotify );
;;;5099   
;;;5100               /* If the task is in the blocked state specifically to wait for a
;;;5101                * notification then unblock it now. */
;;;5102               if( ucOriginalNotifyState == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
000056  2901              CMP      r1,#1
000058  d003              BEQ      |L48.98|
00005a  e016              B        |L48.138|
                  |L48.92|
00005c  f04f0800          MOV      r8,#0                 ;5078
000060  e013              B        |L48.138|
                  |L48.98|
;;;5103               {
;;;5104                   /* The task should not have been on an event list. */
;;;5105                   configASSERT( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == NULL );
;;;5106   
;;;5107                   if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000062  4e14              LDR      r6,|L48.180|
000064  6b70              LDR      r0,[r6,#0x34]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000066  2701              MOVS     r7,#1                 ;5019
000068  b1a0              CBZ      r0,|L48.148|
;;;5108                   {
;;;5109                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
;;;5110                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
;;;5111                   }
;;;5112                   else
;;;5113                   {
;;;5114                       /* The delayed and ready lists cannot be accessed, so hold
;;;5115                        * this task pending until the scheduler is resumed. */
;;;5116                       vListInsertEnd( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
00006a  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
00006e  4812              LDR      r0,|L48.184|
                  |L48.112|
000070  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;5117                   }
;;;5118   
;;;5119                   if( pxTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000074  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000076  6831              LDR      r1,[r6,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000078  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00007a  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00007c  d905              BLS      |L48.138|
;;;5120                   {
;;;5121                       /* The notified task has a priority above the currently
;;;5122                        * executing task so a yield is required. */
;;;5123                       if( pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken != NULL )
00007e  f1b90f00          CMP      r9,#0
000082  d001              BEQ      |L48.136|
;;;5124                       {
;;;5125                           *pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken = pdTRUE;
000084  f8c97000          STR      r7,[r9,#0]
                  |L48.136|
;;;5126                       }
;;;5127   
;;;5128                       /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not
;;;5129                        * using the "xHigherPriorityTaskWoken" parameter to an ISR
;;;5130                        * safe FreeRTOS function. */
;;;5131                       xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000088  61f7              STR      r7,[r6,#0x1c]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L48.138|
00008a  f3858811          MSR      BASEPRI,r5
;;;5132                   }
;;;5133                   else
;;;5134                   {
;;;5135                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;5136                   }
;;;5137               }
;;;5138           }
;;;5139           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
;;;5140   
;;;5141           return xReturn;
00008e  4640              MOV      r0,r8
;;;5142       }
000090  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
                  |L48.148|
000094  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4              ;5109
000096  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
00009a  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]         ;5110
00009c  6932              LDR      r2,[r6,#0x10]         ;5110  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00009e  fa07f100          LSL      r1,r7,r0              ;5110
0000a2  4311              ORRS     r1,r1,r2              ;5110
0000a4  6131              STR      r1,[r6,#0x10]         ;5110  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000a6  4905              LDR      r1,|L48.188|
0000a8  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2       ;5110
0000ac  eb010080          ADD      r0,r1,r0,LSL #2       ;5110
0000b0  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4              ;5110
0000b2  e7dd              B        |L48.112|
;;;5143   
                          ENDP

                  |L48.180|
                          DCD      ||.data||
                  |L48.184|
                          DCD      ||.bss||+0x154
                  |L48.188|
                          DCD      ||.bss||

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotifyStateClear||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotifyStateClear PROC
;;;5237   
;;;5238       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotifyStateClear( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;5239                                                UBaseType_t uxIndexToClear )
;;;5240       {
000002  460d              MOV      r5,r1
000004  b160              CBZ      r0,|L49.32|
;;;5241           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;5242           BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;5243   
;;;5244           configASSERT( uxIndexToClear < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;5245   
;;;5246           /* If null is passed in here then it is the calling task that is having
;;;5247            * its notification state cleared. */
;;;5248           pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000006  4604              MOV      r4,r0
                  |L49.8|
;;;5249   
;;;5250           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000008  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;5251           {
;;;5252               if( pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToClear ] == taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
00000c  1960              ADDS     r0,r4,r5
00000e  f890105c          LDRB     r1,[r0,#0x5c]
000012  2902              CMP      r1,#2
000014  d007              BEQ      |L49.38|
;;;5253               {
;;;5254                   pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToClear ] = taskNOT_WAITING_NOTIFICATION;
;;;5255                   xReturn = pdPASS;
;;;5256               }
;;;5257               else
;;;5258               {
;;;5259                   xReturn = pdFAIL;
000016  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
                  |L49.24|
;;;5260               }
;;;5261           }
;;;5262           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000018  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;5263   
;;;5264           return xReturn;
00001c  4620              MOV      r0,r4
;;;5265       }
00001e  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
                  |L49.32|
000020  4803              LDR      r0,|L49.48|
000022  6804              LDR      r4,[r0,#0]            ;5248  ; pxCurrentTCB
000024  e7f0              B        |L49.8|
                  |L49.38|
000026  2100              MOVS     r1,#0                 ;5254
000028  f880105c          STRB     r1,[r0,#0x5c]         ;5254
00002c  2401              MOVS     r4,#1                 ;5255
00002e  e7f3              B        |L49.24|
;;;5266   
                          ENDP

                  |L49.48|
                          DCD      ||.data||

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotifyWait||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotifyWait PROC
;;;4800   
;;;4801       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotifyWait( UBaseType_t uxIndexToWait,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;4802                                          uint32_t ulBitsToClearOnEntry,
;;;4803                                          uint32_t ulBitsToClearOnExit,
;;;4804                                          uint32_t * pulNotificationValue,
;;;4805                                          TickType_t xTicksToWait )
;;;4806       {
000004  461e              MOV      r6,r3
000006  4691              MOV      r9,r2
000008  460f              MOV      r7,r1
00000a  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;4807           BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;4808   
;;;4809           configASSERT( uxIndexToWait < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;4810   
;;;4811           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
00000c  f8dd8020          LDR      r8,[sp,#0x20]
000010  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;4812           {
;;;4813               /* Only block if a notification is not already pending. */
;;;4814               if( pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] != taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
000014  4d21              LDR      r5,|L50.156|
000016  6828              LDR      r0,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000018  4420              ADD      r0,r0,r4
00001a  f890005c          LDRB     r0,[r0,#0x5c]
00001e  2802              CMP      r0,#2
000020  d018              BEQ      |L50.84|
;;;4815               {
;;;4816                   /* Clear bits in the task's notification value as bits may get
;;;4817                    * set  by the notifying task or interrupt.  This can be used to
;;;4818                    * clear the value to zero. */
;;;4819                   pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ] &= ~ulBitsToClearOnEntry;
000022  6828              LDR      r0,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000024  eb000084          ADD      r0,r0,r4,LSL #2
000028  6d81              LDR      r1,[r0,#0x58]
00002a  43b9              BICS     r1,r1,r7
00002c  6581              STR      r1,[r0,#0x58]
;;;4820   
;;;4821                   /* Mark this task as waiting for a notification. */
;;;4822                   pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] = taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION;
00002e  6829              LDR      r1,[r5,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000030  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000032  4421              ADD      r1,r1,r4
000034  f881005c          STRB     r0,[r1,#0x5c]
;;;4823   
;;;4824                   if( xTicksToWait > ( TickType_t ) 0 )
000038  f1b80000          SUBS     r0,r8,#0
00003c  d00a              BEQ      |L50.84|
;;;4825                   {
;;;4826                       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToWait, pdTRUE );
00003e  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000040  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;4827                       traceTASK_NOTIFY_WAIT_BLOCK( uxIndexToWait );
;;;4828   
;;;4829                       /* All ports are written to allow a yield in a critical
;;;4830                        * section (some will yield immediately, others wait until the
;;;4831                        * critical section exits) - but it is not something that
;;;4832                        * application code should ever do. */
